# CLI Reference — adk cli  documentation

CLI Reference[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cli-reference "Link to this heading")

====================================================================================================================

This page contains the auto-generated command-line reference for the adk tool.

adk[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#adk "Link to this heading")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Agent Development Kit CLI tools.

Usage

adk \[OPTIONS\] COMMAND \[ARGS\]...

Options

\--version[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-version "Link to this definition")

Show the version and exit.

### api\_server[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#adk-api-server "Link to this heading")

Starts a FastAPI server for agents.

AGENTS\_DIR: The directory of agents, where each sub-directory is a single agent, containing at least \_\_init\_\_.py and agent.py files.

Example:

> adk api\_server –session\_service\_uri=\[uri\] –port=\[port\] path/to/agents\_dir

Usage

adk api\_server \[OPTIONS\] \[AGENTS\_DIR\]

Options

\--host <host>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-api_server-host "Link to this definition")

Optional. The binding host of the server

Default:

`'127.0.0.1'`

\--port <port>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-api_server-port "Link to this definition")

Optional. The port of the server

\--allow\_origins <allow\_origins>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-api_server-allow_origins "Link to this definition")

Optional. Any additional origins to allow for CORS.

\-v, \--verbose[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-api_server-v "Link to this definition")

Enable verbose (DEBUG) logging. Shortcut for –log\_level DEBUG.

Default:

`False`

\--log\_level <log\_level>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-api_server-log_level "Link to this definition")

Optional. Set the logging level

Options:

DEBUG | INFO | WARNING | ERROR | CRITICAL

\--trace\_to\_cloud[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-api_server-trace_to_cloud "Link to this definition")

Optional. Whether to enable cloud trace for telemetry.

Default:

`False`

\--otel\_to\_cloud[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-api_server-otel_to_cloud "Link to this definition")

EXPERIMENTAL Optional. Whether to write OTel data to Google Cloud Observability services - Cloud Trace and Cloud Logging.

Default:

`False`

\--reload, \--no-reload[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-api_server-reload "Link to this definition")

Optional. Whether to enable auto reload for server. Not supported for Cloud Run.

\--a2a[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-api_server-a2a "Link to this definition")

Optional. Whether to enable A2A endpoint.

Default:

`False`

\--reload\_agents[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-api_server-reload_agents "Link to this definition")

Optional. Whether to enable live reload for agents changes.

Default:

`False`

\--eval\_storage\_uri <eval\_storage\_uri>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-api_server-eval_storage_uri "Link to this definition")

Optional. The evals storage URI to store agent evals, supported URIs: gs://<bucket name>.

\--extra\_plugins <extra\_plugins>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-api_server-extra_plugins "Link to this definition")

Optional. Comma-separated list of extra plugin classes or instances to enable (e.g., my.module.MyPluginClass or my.module.my\_plugin\_instance).

\--url\_prefix <url\_prefix>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-api_server-url_prefix "Link to this definition")

Optional. URL path prefix when the application is mounted behind a reverse proxy or API gateway (e.g., ‘/api/v1’, ‘/adk’). This ensures generated URLs and redirects work correctly when the app is not served at the root path. Must start with ‘/’ if provided.

\--session\_service\_uri <session\_service\_uri>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-api_server-session_service_uri "Link to this definition")

Optional. The URI of the session service. - Use ‘agentengine://<agent\_engine>’ to connect to Agent Engine

> sessions. <agent\_engine> can either be the full qualified resource name ‘projects/abc/locations/us-central1/reasoningEngines/123’ or the resource id ‘123’.

*   Use ‘sqlite://<path\_to\_sqlite\_file>’ to connect to an aio-sqlite based session service, which is good for local development.
    
*   Use ‘postgresql://<user>:<password>@<host>:<port>/<database\_name>’ to connect to a PostgreSQL DB.
    
*   See [https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/20/core/engines.html#backend-specific-urls](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/20/core/engines.html#backend-specific-urls)
     for more details on other database URIs supported by SQLAlchemy.
    

\--artifact\_service\_uri <artifact\_service\_uri>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-api_server-artifact_service_uri "Link to this definition")

Optional. The URI of the artifact service, supported URIs: gs://<bucket name> for GCS artifact service.

\--memory\_service\_uri <memory\_service\_uri>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-api_server-memory_service_uri "Link to this definition")

Optional. The URI of the memory service. - Use ‘rag://<rag\_corpus\_id>’ to connect to Vertex AI Rag Memory Service. - Use ‘agentengine://<agent\_engine>’ to connect to Agent Engine

> sessions. <agent\_engine> can either be the full qualified resource name ‘projects/abc/locations/us-central1/reasoningEngines/123’ or the resource id ‘123’.

\--session\_db\_url <session\_db\_url>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-api_server-session_db_url "Link to this definition")

Deprecated. Use –session\_service\_uri instead.

\--artifact\_storage\_uri <artifact\_storage\_uri>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-api_server-artifact_storage_uri "Link to this definition")

Deprecated. Use –artifact\_service\_uri instead.

Arguments

AGENTS\_DIR[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-api_server-arg-AGENTS_DIR "Link to this definition")

Optional argument

### conformance[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#adk-conformance "Link to this heading")

Conformance testing tools for ADK.

Usage

adk conformance \[OPTIONS\] COMMAND \[ARGS\]...

#### record[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#adk-conformance-record "Link to this heading")

Generate ADK conformance test YAML files from TestCaseInput specifications.

NOTE: this is work in progress.

This command reads TestCaseInput specifications from input.yaml files, executes the specified test cases against agents, and generates conformance test files with recorded agent interactions as test.yaml files.

Expected directory structure: category/name/input.yaml (TestCaseInput) -> category/name/test.yaml (TestCase)

PATHS: One or more directories containing test case specifications. If no paths are provided, defaults to ‘tests/’ directory.

Examples:

Use default directory: adk conformance record

Custom directories: adk conformance record tests/core tests/tools

Usage

adk conformance record \[OPTIONS\] \[PATHS\]...

Arguments

PATHS[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-conformance-record-arg-PATHS "Link to this definition")

Optional argument(s)

#### test[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#adk-conformance-test "Link to this heading")

Run conformance tests to verify agent behavior consistency.

Validates that agents produce consistent outputs by comparing against recorded interactions or evaluating live execution results.

PATHS can be any number of folder paths. Each folder can either: - Contain a spec.yaml file directly (single test case) - Contain subdirectories with spec.yaml files (multiple test cases)

If no paths are provided, defaults to searching the ‘tests’ folder.

TEST MODES:

replay : Verifies agent interactions match previously recorded behaviors

exactly. Compares LLM requests/responses and tool calls/results.

live : Runs evaluation-based verification (not yet implemented)

DIRECTORY STRUCTURE:

Test cases must follow this structure:

category/

test\_name/

spec.yaml # Test specification

generated-recordings.yaml # Recorded interactions (replay mode)

generated-session.yaml # Session data (replay mode)

EXAMPLES:

\# Run all tests in current directory’s ‘tests’ folder

adk conformance test

\# Run tests from specific folders

adk conformance test tests/core tests/tools

\# Run a single test case

adk conformance test tests/core/description\_001

\# Run in live mode (when available)

adk conformance test –mode=live tests/core

Usage

adk conformance test \[OPTIONS\] \[PATHS\]...

Options

\--mode <mode>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-conformance-test-mode "Link to this definition")

Test mode: ‘replay’ verifies against recorded interactions, ‘live’ runs evaluation-based verification.

Default:

`'replay'`

Options:

replay | live

Arguments

PATHS[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-conformance-test-arg-PATHS "Link to this definition")

Optional argument(s)

### create[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#adk-create "Link to this heading")

Creates a new app in the current folder with prepopulated agent template.

APP\_NAME: required, the folder of the agent source code.

Example:

> adk create path/to/my\_app

Usage

adk create \[OPTIONS\] APP\_NAME

Options

\--model <model>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-create-model "Link to this definition")

Optional. The model used for the root agent.

\--api\_key <api\_key>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-create-api_key "Link to this definition")

Optional. The API Key needed to access the model, e.g. Google AI API Key.

\--project <project>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-create-project "Link to this definition")

Optional. The Google Cloud Project for using VertexAI as backend.

\--region <region>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-create-region "Link to this definition")

Optional. The Google Cloud Region for using VertexAI as backend.

Arguments

APP\_NAME[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-create-arg-APP_NAME "Link to this definition")

Required argument

### deploy[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#adk-deploy "Link to this heading")

Deploys agent to hosted environments.

Usage

adk deploy \[OPTIONS\] COMMAND \[ARGS\]...

#### agent\_engine[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#adk-deploy-agent-engine "Link to this heading")

Deploys an agent to Agent Engine.

Example:

> \# With Express Mode API Key adk deploy agent\_engine –api\_key=\[api\_key\] my\_agent
> 
> \# With Google Cloud Project and Region adk deploy agent\_engine –project=\[project\] –region=\[region\]
> 
> > –staging\_bucket=\[staging\_bucket\] –display\_name=\[app\_name\] my\_agent

Usage

adk deploy agent\_engine \[OPTIONS\] AGENT

Options

\--api\_key <api\_key>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-deploy-agent_engine-api_key "Link to this definition")

Optional. The API key to use for Express Mode. If not provided, the API key from the GOOGLE\_API\_KEY environment variable will be used. It will only be used if GOOGLE\_GENAI\_USE\_VERTEXAI is true. (It will override GOOGLE\_API\_KEY in the .env file if it exists.)

\--project <project>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-deploy-agent_engine-project "Link to this definition")

Optional. Google Cloud project to deploy the agent. It will override GOOGLE\_CLOUD\_PROJECT in the .env file (if it exists). It will be ignored if api\_key is set.

\--region <region>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-deploy-agent_engine-region "Link to this definition")

Optional. Google Cloud region to deploy the agent. It will override GOOGLE\_CLOUD\_LOCATION in the .env file (if it exists). It will be ignored if api\_key is set.

\--staging\_bucket <staging\_bucket>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-deploy-agent_engine-staging_bucket "Link to this definition")

Optional. GCS bucket for staging the deployment artifacts. It will be ignored if api\_key is set.

\--agent\_engine\_id <agent\_engine\_id>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-deploy-agent_engine-agent_engine_id "Link to this definition")

Optional. ID of the Agent Engine instance to update if it exists (default: None, which means a new instance will be created). If project and region are set, this should be the resource ID, and the corresponding resource name in Agent Engine will be: projects/{project}/locations/{region}/reasoningEngines/{agent\_engine\_id}. If api\_key is set, then agent\_engine\_id is required to be the full resource name (i.e. projects/\*/locations/\*/reasoningEngines/\*).

\--trace\_to\_cloud, \--no-trace\_to\_cloud[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-deploy-agent_engine-trace_to_cloud "Link to this definition")

Optional. Whether to enable Cloud Trace for Agent Engine.

\--display\_name <display\_name>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-deploy-agent_engine-display_name "Link to this definition")

Optional. Display name of the agent in Agent Engine.

Default:

`''`

\--description <description>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-deploy-agent_engine-description "Link to this definition")

Optional. Description of the agent in Agent Engine.

Default:

`''`

\--adk\_app <adk\_app>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-deploy-agent_engine-adk_app "Link to this definition")

Optional. Python file for defining the ADK application (default: a file named agent\_engine\_app.py)

\--temp\_folder <temp\_folder>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-deploy-agent_engine-temp_folder "Link to this definition")

Optional. Temp folder for the generated Agent Engine source files. If the folder already exists, its contents will be removed. (default: a timestamped folder in the current working directory).

\--adk\_app\_object <adk\_app\_object>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-deploy-agent_engine-adk_app_object "Link to this definition")

Optional. Python object corresponding to the root ADK agent or app. It can only be root\_agent or app. (default: root\_agent)

\--env\_file <env\_file>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-deploy-agent_engine-env_file "Link to this definition")

Optional. The filepath to the .env file for environment variables. (default: the .env file in the agent directory, if any.)

\--requirements\_file <requirements\_file>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-deploy-agent_engine-requirements_file "Link to this definition")

Optional. The filepath to the requirements.txt file to use. (default: the requirements.txt file in the agent directory, if any.)

\--absolutize\_imports <absolutize\_imports>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-deploy-agent_engine-absolutize_imports "Link to this definition")

NOTE: This flag is deprecated and will be removed in the future.

\--agent\_engine\_config\_file <agent\_engine\_config\_file>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-deploy-agent_engine-agent_engine_config_file "Link to this definition")

Optional. The filepath to the .agent\_engine\_config.json file to use. The values in this file will be overridden by the values set by other flags. (default: the .agent\_engine\_config.json file in the agent directory, if any.)

Arguments

AGENT[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-deploy-agent_engine-arg-AGENT "Link to this definition")

Required argument

#### cloud\_run[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#adk-deploy-cloud-run "Link to this heading")

Deploys an agent to Cloud Run.

AGENT: The path to the agent source code folder.

Use ‘–’ to separate gcloud arguments from adk arguments.

Examples:

> adk deploy cloud\_run –project=\[project\] –region=\[region\] path/to/my\_agent
> 
> adk deploy cloud\_run –project=\[project\] –region=\[region\] path/to/my\_agent
> 
> – –no-allow-unauthenticated –min-instances=2

Usage

adk deploy cloud\_run \[OPTIONS\] AGENT

Options

\--project <project>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-deploy-cloud_run-project "Link to this definition")

Required. Google Cloud project to deploy the agent. When absent, default project from gcloud config is used.

\--region <region>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-deploy-cloud_run-region "Link to this definition")

Required. Google Cloud region to deploy the agent. When absent, gcloud run deploy will prompt later.

\--service\_name <service\_name>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-deploy-cloud_run-service_name "Link to this definition")

Optional. The service name to use in Cloud Run (default: ‘adk-default-service-name’).

\--app\_name <app\_name>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-deploy-cloud_run-app_name "Link to this definition")

Optional. App name of the ADK API server (default: the folder name of the AGENT source code).

\--port <port>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-deploy-cloud_run-port "Link to this definition")

Optional. The port of the ADK API server (default: 8000).

\--trace\_to\_cloud[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-deploy-cloud_run-trace_to_cloud "Link to this definition")

Optional. Whether to enable Cloud Trace for cloud run.

Default:

`False`

\--with\_ui[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-deploy-cloud_run-with_ui "Link to this definition")

Optional. Deploy ADK Web UI if set. (default: deploy ADK API server only)

Default:

`False`

\--temp\_folder <temp\_folder>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-deploy-cloud_run-temp_folder "Link to this definition")

Optional. Temp folder for the generated Cloud Run source files (default: a timestamped folder in the system temp directory).

\--log\_level <log\_level>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-deploy-cloud_run-log_level "Link to this definition")

Optional. Set the logging level

Options:

DEBUG | INFO | WARNING | ERROR | CRITICAL

\--verbosity <verbosity>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-deploy-cloud_run-verbosity "Link to this definition")

Deprecated. Use –log\_level instead.

Options:

DEBUG | INFO | WARNING | ERROR | CRITICAL

\--adk\_version <adk\_version>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-deploy-cloud_run-adk_version "Link to this definition")

Optional. The ADK version used in Cloud Run deployment. (default: the version in the dev environment)

Default:

`'1.19.0'`

\--a2a[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-deploy-cloud_run-a2a "Link to this definition")

Optional. Whether to enable A2A endpoint.

Default:

`False`

\--allow\_origins <allow\_origins>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-deploy-cloud_run-allow_origins "Link to this definition")

Optional. Any additional origins to allow for CORS.

\--session\_service\_uri <session\_service\_uri>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-deploy-cloud_run-session_service_uri "Link to this definition")

Optional. The URI of the session service. - Use ‘agentengine://<agent\_engine>’ to connect to Agent Engine

> sessions. <agent\_engine> can either be the full qualified resource name ‘projects/abc/locations/us-central1/reasoningEngines/123’ or the resource id ‘123’.

*   Use ‘sqlite://<path\_to\_sqlite\_file>’ to connect to an aio-sqlite based session service, which is good for local development.
    
*   Use ‘postgresql://<user>:<password>@<host>:<port>/<database\_name>’ to connect to a PostgreSQL DB.
    
*   See [https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/20/core/engines.html#backend-specific-urls](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/20/core/engines.html#backend-specific-urls)
     for more details on other database URIs supported by SQLAlchemy.
    

\--artifact\_service\_uri <artifact\_service\_uri>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-deploy-cloud_run-artifact_service_uri "Link to this definition")

Optional. The URI of the artifact service, supported URIs: gs://<bucket name> for GCS artifact service.

\--memory\_service\_uri <memory\_service\_uri>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-deploy-cloud_run-memory_service_uri "Link to this definition")

Optional. The URI of the memory service. - Use ‘rag://<rag\_corpus\_id>’ to connect to Vertex AI Rag Memory Service. - Use ‘agentengine://<agent\_engine>’ to connect to Agent Engine

> sessions. <agent\_engine> can either be the full qualified resource name ‘projects/abc/locations/us-central1/reasoningEngines/123’ or the resource id ‘123’.

\--session\_db\_url <session\_db\_url>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-deploy-cloud_run-session_db_url "Link to this definition")

Deprecated. Use –session\_service\_uri instead.

\--artifact\_storage\_uri <artifact\_storage\_uri>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-deploy-cloud_run-artifact_storage_uri "Link to this definition")

Deprecated. Use –artifact\_service\_uri instead.

Arguments

AGENT[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-deploy-cloud_run-arg-AGENT "Link to this definition")

Required argument

#### gke[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#adk-deploy-gke "Link to this heading")

Deploys an agent to GKE.

AGENT: The path to the agent source code folder.

Example:

> adk deploy gke –project=\[project\] –region=\[region\]
> 
> –cluster\_name=\[cluster\_name\] path/to/my\_agent

Usage

adk deploy gke \[OPTIONS\] AGENT

Options

\--project <project>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-deploy-gke-project "Link to this definition")

Required. Google Cloud project to deploy the agent. When absent, default project from gcloud config is used.

\--region <region>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-deploy-gke-region "Link to this definition")

Required. Google Cloud region to deploy the agent. When absent, gcloud run deploy will prompt later.

\--cluster\_name <cluster\_name>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-deploy-gke-cluster_name "Link to this definition")

Required. The name of the GKE cluster.

\--service\_name <service\_name>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-deploy-gke-service_name "Link to this definition")

Optional. The service name to use in GKE (default: ‘adk-default-service-name’).

\--app\_name <app\_name>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-deploy-gke-app_name "Link to this definition")

Optional. App name of the ADK API server (default: the folder name of the AGENT source code).

\--port <port>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-deploy-gke-port "Link to this definition")

Optional. The port of the ADK API server (default: 8000).

\--trace\_to\_cloud[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-deploy-gke-trace_to_cloud "Link to this definition")

Optional. Whether to enable Cloud Trace for GKE.

Default:

`False`

\--with\_ui[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-deploy-gke-with_ui "Link to this definition")

Optional. Deploy ADK Web UI if set. (default: deploy ADK API server only)

Default:

`False`

\--log\_level <log\_level>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-deploy-gke-log_level "Link to this definition")

Optional. Set the logging level

Options:

DEBUG | INFO | WARNING | ERROR | CRITICAL

\--temp\_folder <temp\_folder>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-deploy-gke-temp_folder "Link to this definition")

Optional. Temp folder for the generated GKE source files (default: a timestamped folder in the system temp directory).

\--adk\_version <adk\_version>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-deploy-gke-adk_version "Link to this definition")

Optional. The ADK version used in GKE deployment. (default: the version in the dev environment)

Default:

`'1.19.0'`

\--session\_service\_uri <session\_service\_uri>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-deploy-gke-session_service_uri "Link to this definition")

Optional. The URI of the session service. - Use ‘agentengine://<agent\_engine>’ to connect to Agent Engine

> sessions. <agent\_engine> can either be the full qualified resource name ‘projects/abc/locations/us-central1/reasoningEngines/123’ or the resource id ‘123’.

*   Use ‘sqlite://<path\_to\_sqlite\_file>’ to connect to an aio-sqlite based session service, which is good for local development.
    
*   Use ‘postgresql://<user>:<password>@<host>:<port>/<database\_name>’ to connect to a PostgreSQL DB.
    
*   See [https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/20/core/engines.html#backend-specific-urls](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/20/core/engines.html#backend-specific-urls)
     for more details on other database URIs supported by SQLAlchemy.
    

\--artifact\_service\_uri <artifact\_service\_uri>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-deploy-gke-artifact_service_uri "Link to this definition")

Optional. The URI of the artifact service, supported URIs: gs://<bucket name> for GCS artifact service.

\--memory\_service\_uri <memory\_service\_uri>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-deploy-gke-memory_service_uri "Link to this definition")

Optional. The URI of the memory service. - Use ‘rag://<rag\_corpus\_id>’ to connect to Vertex AI Rag Memory Service. - Use ‘agentengine://<agent\_engine>’ to connect to Agent Engine

> sessions. <agent\_engine> can either be the full qualified resource name ‘projects/abc/locations/us-central1/reasoningEngines/123’ or the resource id ‘123’.

Arguments

AGENT[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-deploy-gke-arg-AGENT "Link to this definition")

Required argument

### eval[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#adk-eval "Link to this heading")

Evaluates an agent given the eval sets.

AGENT\_MODULE\_FILE\_PATH: The path to the \_\_init\_\_.py file that contains a module by the name “agent”. “agent” module contains a root\_agent.

EVAL\_SET\_FILE\_PATH\_OR\_ID: You can specify one or more eval set file paths or eval set id.

Mixing of eval set file paths with eval set ids is not allowed.

_Eval Set File Path_ For each file, all evals will be run by default.

If you want to run only specific evals from an eval set, first create a comma separated list of eval names and then add that as a suffix to the eval set file name, demarcated by a :.

For example, we have sample\_eval\_set\_file.json file that has following the eval cases: sample\_eval\_set\_file.json:

> [|](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#id1)
> ……. eval\_1 [|](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#id3)
> ……. eval\_2 [|](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#id5)
> ……. eval\_3 [|](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#id7)
> ……. eval\_4 [|](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#id9)
> ……. eval\_5

sample\_eval\_set\_file.json:eval\_1,eval\_2,eval\_3

This will only run eval\_1, eval\_2 and eval\_3 from sample\_eval\_set\_file.json.

_Eval Set ID_ For each eval set, all evals will be run by default.

If you want to run only specific evals from an eval set, first create a comma separated list of eval names and then add that as a suffix to the eval set file name, demarcated by a :.

For example, we have sample\_eval\_set\_id that has following the eval cases: sample\_eval\_set\_id:

> [|](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#id11)
> ……. eval\_1 [|](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#id13)
> ……. eval\_2 [|](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#id15)
> ……. eval\_3 [|](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#id17)
> ……. eval\_4 [|](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#id19)
> ……. eval\_5

If we did:

sample\_eval\_set\_id:eval\_1,eval\_2,eval\_3

This will only run eval\_1, eval\_2 and eval\_3 from sample\_eval\_set\_id.

CONFIG\_FILE\_PATH: The path to config file.

PRINT\_DETAILED\_RESULTS: Prints detailed results on the console.

Usage

adk eval \[OPTIONS\] AGENT\_MODULE\_FILE\_PATH \[EVAL\_SET\_FILE\_PATH\_OR\_ID\]...

Options

\--config\_file\_path <config\_file\_path>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-eval-config_file_path "Link to this definition")

Optional. The path to config file.

\--print\_detailed\_results[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-eval-print_detailed_results "Link to this definition")

Optional. Whether to print detailed results on console or not.

Default:

`False`

\--eval\_storage\_uri <eval\_storage\_uri>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-eval-eval_storage_uri "Link to this definition")

Optional. The evals storage URI to store agent evals, supported URIs: gs://<bucket name>.

\--log\_level <log\_level>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-eval-log_level "Link to this definition")

Optional. Set the logging level

Options:

DEBUG | INFO | WARNING | ERROR | CRITICAL

Arguments

AGENT\_MODULE\_FILE\_PATH[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-eval-arg-AGENT_MODULE_FILE_PATH "Link to this definition")

Required argument

EVAL\_SET\_FILE\_PATH\_OR\_ID[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-eval-arg-EVAL_SET_FILE_PATH_OR_ID "Link to this definition")

Optional argument(s)

### eval\_set[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#adk-eval-set "Link to this heading")

Manage Eval Sets.

Usage

adk eval\_set \[OPTIONS\] COMMAND \[ARGS\]...

#### add\_eval\_case[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#adk-eval-set-add-eval-case "Link to this heading")

Adds eval cases to the given eval set.

There are several ways that an eval case can be created, for now this method only supports adding one using a conversation scenarios file.

If an eval case for the generated id already exists, then we skip adding it.

Usage

adk eval\_set add\_eval\_case \[OPTIONS\] AGENT\_MODULE\_FILE\_PATH EVAL\_SET\_ID

Options

\--scenarios\_file <scenarios\_file>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-eval_set-add_eval_case-scenarios_file "Link to this definition")

**Required** A path to file containing JSON serialized ConversationScenarios.

\--session\_input\_file <session\_input\_file>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-eval_set-add_eval_case-session_input_file "Link to this definition")

**Required** Path to session file containing SessionInput in JSON format.

\--eval\_storage\_uri <eval\_storage\_uri>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-eval_set-add_eval_case-eval_storage_uri "Link to this definition")

Optional. The evals storage URI to store agent evals, supported URIs: gs://<bucket name>.

\--log\_level <log\_level>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-eval_set-add_eval_case-log_level "Link to this definition")

Optional. Set the logging level

Options:

DEBUG | INFO | WARNING | ERROR | CRITICAL

Arguments

AGENT\_MODULE\_FILE\_PATH[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-eval_set-add_eval_case-arg-AGENT_MODULE_FILE_PATH "Link to this definition")

Required argument

EVAL\_SET\_ID[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-eval_set-add_eval_case-arg-EVAL_SET_ID "Link to this definition")

Required argument

#### create[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#adk-eval-set-create "Link to this heading")

Creates an empty EvalSet given the agent\_module\_file\_path and eval\_set\_id.

Usage

adk eval\_set create \[OPTIONS\] AGENT\_MODULE\_FILE\_PATH EVAL\_SET\_ID

Options

\--eval\_storage\_uri <eval\_storage\_uri>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-eval_set-create-eval_storage_uri "Link to this definition")

Optional. The evals storage URI to store agent evals, supported URIs: gs://<bucket name>.

\--log\_level <log\_level>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-eval_set-create-log_level "Link to this definition")

Optional. Set the logging level

Options:

DEBUG | INFO | WARNING | ERROR | CRITICAL

Arguments

AGENT\_MODULE\_FILE\_PATH[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-eval_set-create-arg-AGENT_MODULE_FILE_PATH "Link to this definition")

Required argument

EVAL\_SET\_ID[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-eval_set-create-arg-EVAL_SET_ID "Link to this definition")

Required argument

### run[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#adk-run "Link to this heading")

Runs an interactive CLI for a certain agent.

AGENT: The path to the agent source code folder.

Example:

> adk run path/to/my\_agent

Usage

adk run \[OPTIONS\] AGENT

Options

\--save\_session[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-run-save_session "Link to this definition")

Optional. Whether to save the session to a json file on exit.

Default:

`False`

\--session\_id <session\_id>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-run-session_id "Link to this definition")

Optional. The session ID to save the session to on exit when –save\_session is set to true. User will be prompted to enter a session ID if not set.

\--replay <replay>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-run-replay "Link to this definition")

The json file that contains the initial state of the session and user queries. A new session will be created using this state. And user queries are run against the newly created session. Users cannot continue to interact with the agent.

\--resume <resume>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-run-resume "Link to this definition")

The json file that contains a previously saved session (by –save\_session option). The previous session will be re-displayed. And user can continue to interact with the agent.

Arguments

AGENT[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-run-arg-AGENT "Link to this definition")

Required argument

### web[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#adk-web "Link to this heading")

Starts a FastAPI server with Web UI for agents.

AGENTS\_DIR: The directory of agents, where each sub-directory is a single agent, containing at least \_\_init\_\_.py and agent.py files.

Example:

> adk web –session\_service\_uri=\[uri\] –port=\[port\] path/to/agents\_dir

Usage

adk web \[OPTIONS\] \[AGENTS\_DIR\]

Options

\--host <host>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-web-host "Link to this definition")

Optional. The binding host of the server

Default:

`'127.0.0.1'`

\--port <port>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-web-port "Link to this definition")

Optional. The port of the server

\--allow\_origins <allow\_origins>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-web-allow_origins "Link to this definition")

Optional. Any additional origins to allow for CORS.

\-v, \--verbose[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-web-v "Link to this definition")

Enable verbose (DEBUG) logging. Shortcut for –log\_level DEBUG.

Default:

`False`

\--log\_level <log\_level>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-web-log_level "Link to this definition")

Optional. Set the logging level

Options:

DEBUG | INFO | WARNING | ERROR | CRITICAL

\--trace\_to\_cloud[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-web-trace_to_cloud "Link to this definition")

Optional. Whether to enable cloud trace for telemetry.

Default:

`False`

\--otel\_to\_cloud[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-web-otel_to_cloud "Link to this definition")

EXPERIMENTAL Optional. Whether to write OTel data to Google Cloud Observability services - Cloud Trace and Cloud Logging.

Default:

`False`

\--reload, \--no-reload[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-web-reload "Link to this definition")

Optional. Whether to enable auto reload for server. Not supported for Cloud Run.

\--a2a[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-web-a2a "Link to this definition")

Optional. Whether to enable A2A endpoint.

Default:

`False`

\--reload\_agents[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-web-reload_agents "Link to this definition")

Optional. Whether to enable live reload for agents changes.

Default:

`False`

\--eval\_storage\_uri <eval\_storage\_uri>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-web-eval_storage_uri "Link to this definition")

Optional. The evals storage URI to store agent evals, supported URIs: gs://<bucket name>.

\--extra\_plugins <extra\_plugins>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-web-extra_plugins "Link to this definition")

Optional. Comma-separated list of extra plugin classes or instances to enable (e.g., my.module.MyPluginClass or my.module.my\_plugin\_instance).

\--url\_prefix <url\_prefix>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-web-url_prefix "Link to this definition")

Optional. URL path prefix when the application is mounted behind a reverse proxy or API gateway (e.g., ‘/api/v1’, ‘/adk’). This ensures generated URLs and redirects work correctly when the app is not served at the root path. Must start with ‘/’ if provided.

\--logo-text <logo\_text>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-web-logo-text "Link to this definition")

Optional. The text to display in the logo of the web UI.

\--logo-image-url <logo\_image\_url>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-web-logo-image-url "Link to this definition")

Optional. The URL of the image to display in the logo of the web UI.

\--session\_service\_uri <session\_service\_uri>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-web-session_service_uri "Link to this definition")

Optional. The URI of the session service. - Use ‘agentengine://<agent\_engine>’ to connect to Agent Engine

> sessions. <agent\_engine> can either be the full qualified resource name ‘projects/abc/locations/us-central1/reasoningEngines/123’ or the resource id ‘123’.

*   Use ‘sqlite://<path\_to\_sqlite\_file>’ to connect to an aio-sqlite based session service, which is good for local development.
    
*   Use ‘postgresql://<user>:<password>@<host>:<port>/<database\_name>’ to connect to a PostgreSQL DB.
    
*   See [https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/20/core/engines.html#backend-specific-urls](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/20/core/engines.html#backend-specific-urls)
     for more details on other database URIs supported by SQLAlchemy.
    

\--artifact\_service\_uri <artifact\_service\_uri>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-web-artifact_service_uri "Link to this definition")

Optional. The URI of the artifact service, supported URIs: gs://<bucket name> for GCS artifact service.

\--memory\_service\_uri <memory\_service\_uri>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-web-memory_service_uri "Link to this definition")

Optional. The URI of the memory service. - Use ‘rag://<rag\_corpus\_id>’ to connect to Vertex AI Rag Memory Service. - Use ‘agentengine://<agent\_engine>’ to connect to Agent Engine

> sessions. <agent\_engine> can either be the full qualified resource name ‘projects/abc/locations/us-central1/reasoningEngines/123’ or the resource id ‘123’.

\--session\_db\_url <session\_db\_url>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-web-session_db_url "Link to this definition")

Deprecated. Use –session\_service\_uri instead.

\--artifact\_storage\_uri <artifact\_storage\_uri>[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-web-artifact_storage_uri "Link to this definition")

Deprecated. Use –artifact\_service\_uri instead.

Arguments

AGENTS\_DIR[¶](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/cli.html#cmdoption-adk-web-arg-AGENTS_DIR "Link to this definition")

Optional argument

[adk cli](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/cli/index.html)

==========================================================================

# AgentTool | ADK for TypeScript: API Reference

*   [AgentTool](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/AgentTool.html)
    

Class AgentTool
===============

A tool that wraps an agent.

This tool allows an agent to be called as a tool within a larger application. The agent's input schema is used to define the tool's input parameters, and the agent's output is returned as the tool's result.

#### Param: config:[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/AgentTool.html#param-config)

The configuration of the agent tool.

#### Hierarchy ([View Summary](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/hierarchy.html#AgentTool)
)

*   [BaseTool](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseTool.html)
    *   AgentTool

Constructors
------------

### constructor[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/AgentTool.html#constructor)

*   new AgentTool(config: AgentToolConfig): [AgentTool](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/AgentTool.html)
    [](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/AgentTool.html#constructoragenttool)
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   config: AgentToolConfig
    
    #### Returns [AgentTool](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/AgentTool.html)
    

Properties
----------

### `Readonly`description[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/AgentTool.html#description)

description: string

### `Readonly`isLongRunning[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/AgentTool.html#islongrunning)

isLongRunning: boolean

### `Readonly`name[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/AgentTool.html#name)

name: string

Accessors
---------

### apiVariant[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/AgentTool.html#apivariant)

*   get apiVariant(): GoogleLLMVariant
    
    The Google API LLM variant to use.
    
    #### Returns GoogleLLMVariant
    

Methods
-------

### \_getDeclaration[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/AgentTool.html#_getdeclaration)

*   \_getDeclaration(): FunctionDeclaration[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/AgentTool.html#_getdeclaration-1)
    
    Gets the OpenAPI specification of this tool in the form of a FunctionDeclaration.
    
    NOTE
    
    *   Required if subclass uses the default implementation of `processLlmRequest` to add function declaration to LLM request.
    *   Otherwise, can be skipped, e.g. for a built-in GoogleSearch tool for Gemini.
    
    #### Returns FunctionDeclaration
    
    The FunctionDeclaration of this tool, or undefined if it doesn't need to be added to LlmRequest.config.
    

### processLlmRequest[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/AgentTool.html#processllmrequest)

*   processLlmRequest(request: [ToolProcessLlmRequest](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/ToolProcessLlmRequest.html)
    ): Promise<void\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/AgentTool.html#processllmrequest-1)
    
    Processes the outgoing LLM request for this tool.
    
    Use cases:
    
    *   Most common use case is adding this tool to the LLM request.
    *   Some tools may just preprocess the LLM request before it's sent out.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   request: [ToolProcessLlmRequest](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/ToolProcessLlmRequest.html)
        
        The request to process the LLM request.
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<void\>
    

### runAsync[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/AgentTool.html#runasync)

*   runAsync(request: [RunAsyncToolRequest](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/RunAsyncToolRequest.html)
    ): Promise<unknown\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/AgentTool.html#runasync-1)
    
    Runs the tool with the given arguments and context.
    
    NOTE
    
    *   Required if this tool needs to run at the client side.
    *   Otherwise, can be skipped, e.g. for a built-in GoogleSearch tool for Gemini.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   request: [RunAsyncToolRequest](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/RunAsyncToolRequest.html)
        
        The request to run the tool.
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<unknown\>
    
    A promise that resolves to the tool response.
    

Constructors

Methods
-------

### `Abstract`connect[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseLlm.html#connect)

*   connect(llmRequest: [LlmRequest](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmRequest.html)
    ): Promise<[BaseLlmConnection](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/BaseLlmConnection.html)
    \>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseLlm.html#connect-1)
    
    Creates a live connection to the LLM.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   llmRequest: [LlmRequest](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmRequest.html)
        
        LlmRequest, the request to send to the LLM.

    \>
    
    A live connection to the LLM.
    

### `Abstract`generateContentAsync[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseLlm.html#generatecontentasync)

*   generateContentAsync(  
        llmRequest: [LlmRequest](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmRequest.html)
    ,  
        stream?: boolean,  
    ): AsyncGenerator<[LlmResponse](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html)
    , void\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseLlm.html#generatecontentasync-1)
    
    Generates one content from the given contents and tools.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   llmRequest: [LlmRequest](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmRequest.html)
        
        LlmRequest, the request to send to the LLM.
        
    *   `Optional`stream: boolean
        
        whether to do streaming call. For non-streaming call, it will only yield one Content.
        
    
    #### Returns AsyncGenerator<[LlmResponse](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html)
    , void\>
    
    A generator of LlmResponse.
    

### maybeAppendUserContent[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseLlm.html#maybeappendusercontent)

\# Agent Development Kit > Build powerful multi-agent systems with Agent Development Kit An open-source, code-first toolkit for building, evaluating, and deploying sophisticated AI agents with flexibility and control. ## Build Agents - \[Get started\](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/get-started/index.md) - \[Technical Overview\](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/get-started/about/index.md) - \[Go\](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/get-started/go/index.md) - # BaseAgent | ADK for TypeScript: API Reference

*   [BaseAgent](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseAgent.html)
    

Class BaseAgent`Abstract`
=========================

Base class for all agents in Agent Development Kit.

#### Hierarchy ([View Summary](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/hierarchy.html#BaseAgent)
)

*   BaseAgent
    *   [LlmAgent](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LlmAgent.html)
        
    *   [LoopAgent](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LoopAgent.html)
        
    *   [ParallelAgent](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/ParallelAgent.html)
        
    *   [SequentialAgent](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/SequentialAgent.html)
        

Constructors
------------

### constructor[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseAgent.html#constructor)

*   new BaseAgent(config: BaseAgentConfig): [BaseAgent](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseAgent.html)
    [](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseAgent.html#constructorbaseagent)
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   config: BaseAgentConfig
    
    #### Returns [BaseAgent](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseAgent.html)
    

Properties
----------

### `Readonly`afterAgentCallback[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseAgent.html#afteragentcallback)

afterAgentCallback: SingleAgentCallback\[\]

Callback or list of callbacks to be invoked after the agent run.

When a list of callbacks is provided, the callbacks will be called in the order they are listed until a callback does not return undefined.

#### Param: callbackContext:[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseAgent.html#param-callbackcontext)

MUST be named 'callbackContext' (enforced).

#### Returns[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseAgent.html#returns)

Content: The content to return to the user. When the content is present, the provided content will be used as agent response and appended to event history as agent response.

### `Readonly`beforeAgentCallback[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseAgent.html#beforeagentcallback)

beforeAgentCallback: SingleAgentCallback\[\]

Callback or list of callbacks to be invoked before the agent run.

When a list of callbacks is provided, the callbacks will be called in the order they are listed until a callback does not return undefined.

#### Param: callbackContext:[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseAgent.html#param-callbackcontext-1)

MUST be named 'callbackContext' (enforced).

#### Returns[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseAgent.html#returns-1)

Content: The content to return to the user. When the content is present, the agent run will be skipped and the provided content will be returned to user.

### `Optional` `Readonly`description[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseAgent.html#description)

description?: string

Description about the agent's capability.

The model uses this to determine whether to delegate control to the agent. One-line description is enough and preferred.

### `Readonly`name[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseAgent.html#name)

name: string

The agent's name. Agent name must be a JS identifier and unique within the agent tree. Agent name cannot be "user", since it's reserved for end-user's input.

### `Optional`parentAgent[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseAgent.html#parentagent)

parentAgent?: [BaseAgent](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseAgent.html)

The parent agent of this agent.

Note that an agent can ONLY be added as sub-agent once.

If you want to add one agent twice as sub-agent, consider to create two agent instances with identical config, but with different name and add them to the agent tree.

The parent agent is the agent that created this agent.

### `Readonly`rootAgent[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseAgent.html#rootagent)

rootAgent: [BaseAgent](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseAgent.html)

Root agent of this agent.

### `Readonly`subAgents[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseAgent.html#subagents)

subAgents: [BaseAgent](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseAgent.html)
\[\]

The sub-agents of this agent.

-------

### findAgent[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseAgent.html#findagent)

*   findAgent(name: string): [BaseAgent](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseAgent.html)
     | undefined[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseAgent.html#findagent-1)
    
    Finds the agent with the given name in this agent and its descendants.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   name: string
        
        The name of the agent to find.
        
    
    #### Returns [BaseAgent](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseAgent.html)
     | undefined
    
    The agent with the given name, or undefined if not found.

A toolset is a collection of tools that can be used by an agent.

)

    *   [MCPToolset](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/MCPToolset.html)

    [](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseToolset.html#constructorbasetoolset)
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   toolFilter: string\[\] | ToolPredicate

Properties
----------

### `Readonly`toolFilter[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseToolset.html#toolfilter)

Methods
-------

### `Abstract`close[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseToolset.html#close)

*   close(): Promise<void\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseToolset.html#close-1)
    
    Closes the toolset.
    
    NOTE: This method is invoked, for example, at the end of an agent server's lifecycle or when the toolset is no longer needed. Implementations should ensure that any open connections, files, or other managed resources are properly released to prevent leaks.
    
    #### Returns Promise<void\>
    
    A Promise that resolves when the toolset is closed.
    

### `Abstract`getTools[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseToolset.html#gettools)

*   getTools(context?: ReadonlyContext): Promise<[BaseTool](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseTool.html)
    \[\]\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseToolset.html#gettools-1)
    
    Returns the tools that should be exposed to LLM.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   `Optional`context: ReadonlyContext
        
        Context used to filter tools available to the agent. If not defined, all tools in the toolset are returned.
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<[BaseTool](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseTool.html)
    \[\]\>
    
    A Promise that resolves to the list of tools.
    

### `Protected`isToolSelected[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseToolset.html#istoolselected)

*   isToolSelected(tool: [BaseTool](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseTool.html)
    , context: ReadonlyContext): boolean[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseToolset.html#istoolselected-1)
    
    Returns whether the tool should be exposed to LLM.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   tool: [BaseTool](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseTool.html)
        
        The tool to check.
        
    *   context: ReadonlyContext
        
        Context used to filter tools available to the agent.
        
    
    #### Returns boolean
    
    Whether the tool should be exposed to LLM.
    

### processLlmRequest[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseToolset.html#processllmrequest)

*   processLlmRequest(  
        toolContext: [ToolContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/ToolContext.html)
    ,  
        llmRequest: [LlmRequest](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmRequest.html)
    ,  
    ): Promise<void\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseToolset.html#processllmrequest-1)
    
    Processes the outgoing LLM request for this toolset. This method will be called before each tool processes the llm request.
    
    Use cases:
    
    *   Instead of let each tool process the llm request, we can let the toolset process the llm request. e.g. ComputerUseToolset can add computer use tool to the llm request.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   toolContext: [ToolContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/ToolContext.html)
        
        The context of the tool.
        
    *   llmRequest: [LlmRequest](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmRequest.html)
        
        The outgoing LLM request, mutable this method.
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<void\>

### \[asyncIterator\][](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LiveRequestQueue.html#asynciterator)

    \>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LiveRequestQueue.html#get-1)

    ): void[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LiveRequestQueue.html#send-1)

# MCPSessionManager | ADK for TypeScript: API Reference

*   [MCPSessionManager](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/MCPSessionManager.html)
    

Class MCPSessionManager
=======================

Manages Model Context Protocol (MCP) client sessions.

This class is responsible for establishing and managing connections to MCP servers. It supports different transport protocols like Standard I/O (Stdio) and Server-Sent Events (SSE) over HTTP, determined by the provided connection parameters.

The primary purpose of this manager is to abstract away the details of session creation and connection handling, providing a simple interface for creating new MCP client instances that can be used to interact with remote tools.

Constructors
------------

### constructor[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/MCPSessionManager.html#constructor)

*   new MCPSessionManager(connectionParams: [MCPConnectionParams](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/types/MCPConnectionParams.html)
    ): [MCPSessionManager](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/MCPSessionManager.html)
    [](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/MCPSessionManager.html#constructormcpsessionmanager)
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   connectionParams: [MCPConnectionParams](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/types/MCPConnectionParams.html)
        
    
    #### Returns [MCPSessionManager](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/MCPSessionManager.html)

-------

Methods

### \_getDeclaration[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseTool.html#_getdeclaration)

*   \_getDeclaration(): FunctionDeclaration | undefined[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseTool.html#_getdeclaration-1)
    
    Gets the OpenAPI specification of this tool in the form of a FunctionDeclaration.
    
    NOTE
    
    *   Required if subclass uses the default implementation of `processLlmRequest` to add function declaration to LLM request.
    *   Otherwise, can be skipped, e.g. for a built-in GoogleSearch tool for Gemini.
    
    #### Returns FunctionDeclaration | undefined
    
    The FunctionDeclaration of this tool, or undefined if it doesn't need to be added to LlmRequest.config.
    

### processLlmRequest[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseTool.html#processllmrequest)

*   processLlmRequest(request: [ToolProcessLlmRequest](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/ToolProcessLlmRequest.html)
    ): Promise<void\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseTool.html#processllmrequest-1)
    
    Processes the outgoing LLM request for this tool.
    
    Use cases:
    
    *   Most common use case is adding this tool to the LLM request.
    *   Some tools may just preprocess the LLM request before it's sent out.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   request: [ToolProcessLlmRequest](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/ToolProcessLlmRequest.html)
        
        The request to process the LLM request.
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<void\>
    

### `Abstract`runAsync[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseTool.html#runasync)

*   runAsync(request: [RunAsyncToolRequest](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/RunAsyncToolRequest.html)
    ): Promise<unknown\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseTool.html#runasync-1)
    
    Runs the tool with the given arguments and context.
    
    NOTE
    
    *   Required if this tool needs to run at the client side.
    *   Otherwise, can be skipped, e.g. for a built-in GoogleSearch tool for Gemini.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   request: [RunAsyncToolRequest](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/RunAsyncToolRequest.html)
        
        The request to run the tool.
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<unknown\>
    
    A promise that resolves to the tool response.

# BaseCredentialService | ADK for TypeScript: API Reference

*   [BaseCredentialService](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/BaseCredentialService.html)
    

Interface BaseCredentialService
===============================

Abstract class for Service that loads / saves tool credentials from / to the backend credential store.

interface BaseCredentialService {  
    [loadCredential](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/BaseCredentialService.html#loadcredential-1)
(  
        authConfig: AuthConfig,  
        toolContext: [ToolContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/ToolContext.html)
,  
    ): Promise<AuthCredential | undefined\>;  
    [saveCredential](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/BaseCredentialService.html#savecredential-1)
(  
        authConfig: AuthConfig,  
        toolContext: [ToolContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/ToolContext.html)
,  
    ): Promise<void\>;  
}

Methods
-------

### loadCredential[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/BaseCredentialService.html#loadcredential)

*   loadCredential(  
        authConfig: AuthConfig,  
        toolContext: [ToolContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/ToolContext.html)
    ,  
    ): Promise<AuthCredential | undefined\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/BaseCredentialService.html#loadcredential-1)
    
    Loads the credential by auth config and current tool context from the backend credential store.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   authConfig: AuthConfig
        
        The auth config which contains the auth scheme and auth credential information. auth\_config.get\_credential\_key will be used to build the key to load the credential.
        
    *   toolContext: [ToolContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/ToolContext.html)
        
        The context of the current invocation when the tool is trying to load the credential.
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<AuthCredential | undefined\>
    
    A promise that resolves to the credential saved in the store.
    

### saveCredential[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/BaseCredentialService.html#savecredential)

*   saveCredential(authConfig: AuthConfig, toolContext: [ToolContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/ToolContext.html)
    ): Promise<void\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/BaseCredentialService.html#savecredential-1)

    *   authConfig: AuthConfig
    *   toolContext: [ToolContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/ToolContext.html)
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<void\>

# BaseLlmConnection | ADK for TypeScript: API Reference

*   [BaseLlmConnection](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/BaseLlmConnection.html)
    

Interface BaseLlmConnection
===========================

The base class for a live model connection.

interface BaseLlmConnection {  
    [close](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/BaseLlmConnection.html#close-1)
(): Promise<void\>;  
    [receive](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/BaseLlmConnection.html#receive-1)
(): AsyncGenerator<[LlmResponse](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html)
, void, void\>;  
    [sendContent](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/BaseLlmConnection.html#sendcontent-1)
(content: Content): Promise<void\>;  
    [sendHistory](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/BaseLlmConnection.html#sendhistory-1)
(history: Content\[\]): Promise<void\>;  
    [sendRealtime](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/BaseLlmConnection.html#sendrealtime-1)
(blob: Blob\_2): Promise<void\>;  
}

Methods
-------

### close[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/BaseLlmConnection.html#close)

*   close(): Promise<void\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/BaseLlmConnection.html#close-1)
    
    Closes the llm server connection.
    
    #### Returns Promise<void\>
    

### receive[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/BaseLlmConnection.html#receive)

*   receive(): AsyncGenerator<[LlmResponse](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html)
    , void, void\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/BaseLlmConnection.html#receive-1)
    
    Receives the model response using the llm server connection.
    
    #### Returns AsyncGenerator<[LlmResponse](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html)
    , void, void\>
    
    A generator of LlmResponse.
    

### sendContent[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/BaseLlmConnection.html#sendcontent)

*   sendContent(content: Content): Promise<void\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/BaseLlmConnection.html#sendcontent-1)
    
    Sends the content to the model.
    
    The model will respond immediately upon receiving the content. If you send function responses, all parts in the content should be function responses.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   content: Content
        
        The content to send to the model.
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<void\>
    

### sendHistory[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/BaseLlmConnection.html#sendhistory)

*   sendHistory(history: Content\[\]): Promise<void\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/BaseLlmConnection.html#sendhistory-1)
    
    Sends the conversation history to the model.
    
    You call this method right after setting up the model connection. The model will respond if the last content is from user, otherwise it will wait for new user input before responding.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   history: Content\[\]
        
        The conversation history to send to the model.
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<void\>
    

### sendRealtime[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/BaseLlmConnection.html#sendrealtime)

*   sendRealtime(blob: Blob\_2): Promise<void\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/BaseLlmConnection.html#sendrealtime-1)
    
    Sends a chunk of audio or a frame of video to the model in realtime.
    
    The model may not respond immediately upon receiving the blob. It will do voice activity detection and decide when to respond.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   blob: Blob\_2
        
        The blob to send to the model.
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<void\>
    

Methods

[close](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/BaseLlmConnection.html#close)
[receive](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/BaseLlmConnection.html#receive)
[sendContent](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/BaseLlmConnection.html#sendcontent)
[sendHistory](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/BaseLlmConnection.html#sendhistory)
[sendRealtime](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/BaseLlmConnection.html#sendrealtime)

Request sent to live agents.

    [activityEnd](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LiveRequest.html#activityend)
?: ActivityEnd;  
    [activityStart](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LiveRequest.html#activitystart)
?: ActivityStart;  
    [blob](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LiveRequest.html#blob)
?: Blob\_2;  
    [close](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LiveRequest.html#close)
?: boolean;

?: Content;  
}

Properties
----------

### `Optional`activityEnd[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LiveRequest.html#activityend)

activityEnd?: ActivityEnd

If set, signal the end of user activity to the model.

### `Optional`activityStart[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LiveRequest.html#activitystart)

activityStart?: ActivityStart

If set, signal the start of user activity to the model.

### `Optional`blob[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LiveRequest.html#blob)

blob?: Blob\_2

If set, send the blob to the model in realtime mode.

### `Optional`close[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LiveRequest.html#close)

close?: boolean

If set, close the queue.

### `Optional`content[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LiveRequest.html#content)

content?: Content

If set, send the content to the model in turn-by-turn mode.

    [headers](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/GeminiParams.html#headers)
?: Record<string, string\>;  
    [location](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/GeminiParams.html#location)
?: string;  
    [model](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/GeminiParams.html#model)
?: string;  
    [project](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/GeminiParams.html#project)
?: string;  
    [vertexai](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/GeminiParams.html#vertexai)
?: boolean;  
}

Properties
----------

### `Optional`apiKey[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/GeminiParams.html#apikey)

apiKey?: string

The API key to use for the Gemini API. If not provided, it will look for the GOOGLE\_GENAI\_API\_KEY or GEMINI\_API\_KEY environment variable.

### `Optional`headers[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/GeminiParams.html#headers)

headers?: Record<string, string\>

Headers to merge with internally crafted headers.

### `Optional`location[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/GeminiParams.html#location)

location?: string

The Vertex AI location. Required if `vertexai` is true.

### `Optional`model[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/GeminiParams.html#model)

model?: string

The name of the model to use. Defaults to 'gemini-2.5-flash'.

### `Optional`project[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/GeminiParams.html#project)

project?: string

The Vertex AI project ID. Required if `vertexai` is true.

### `Optional`vertexai[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/GeminiParams.html#vertexai)

vertexai?: boolean

Whether to use Vertex AI. If true, `project`, `location` should be provided.

# InMemoryMemoryService | ADK for TypeScript: API Reference

*   [InMemoryMemoryService](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/InMemoryMemoryService.html)
    

Class InMemoryMemoryService
===========================

An in-memory memory service for prototyping purpose only.

Uses keyword matching instead of semantic search.

*   [BaseMemoryService](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/BaseMemoryService.html)
