# LoopAgent | ADK for TypeScript: API Reference

*   [LoopAgent](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LoopAgent.html)
    

Class LoopAgent
===============

A shell agent that run its sub-agents in a loop.

When sub-agent generates an event with escalate or max\_iterations are reached, the loop agent will stop.

#### Hierarchy ([View Summary](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/hierarchy.html#LoopAgent)
)

*   [BaseAgent](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseAgent.html)
    *   LoopAgent

Constructors
------------

### constructor[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LoopAgent.html#constructor)

*   new LoopAgent(config: LoopAgentConfig): [LoopAgent](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LoopAgent.html)
    [](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LoopAgent.html#constructorloopagent)
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   config: LoopAgentConfig
    
    #### Returns [LoopAgent](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LoopAgent.html)
    

Properties
----------

### `Readonly`afterAgentCallback[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LoopAgent.html#afteragentcallback)

afterAgentCallback: SingleAgentCallback\[\]

Callback or list of callbacks to be invoked after the agent run.

When a list of callbacks is provided, the callbacks will be called in the order they are listed until a callback does not return undefined.

#### Param: callbackContext:[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LoopAgent.html#param-callbackcontext)

MUST be named 'callbackContext' (enforced).

#### Returns[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LoopAgent.html#returns)

Content: The content to return to the user. When the content is present, the provided content will be used as agent response and appended to event history as agent response.

### `Readonly`beforeAgentCallback[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LoopAgent.html#beforeagentcallback)

beforeAgentCallback: SingleAgentCallback\[\]

Callback or list of callbacks to be invoked before the agent run.

When a list of callbacks is provided, the callbacks will be called in the order they are listed until a callback does not return undefined.

#### Param: callbackContext:[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LoopAgent.html#param-callbackcontext-1)

MUST be named 'callbackContext' (enforced).

#### Returns[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LoopAgent.html#returns-1)

Content: The content to return to the user. When the content is present, the agent run will be skipped and the provided content will be returned to user.

### `Optional` `Readonly`description[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LoopAgent.html#description)

description?: string

Description about the agent's capability.

The model uses this to determine whether to delegate control to the agent. One-line description is enough and preferred.

### `Readonly`name[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LoopAgent.html#name)

name: string

The agent's name. Agent name must be a JS identifier and unique within the agent tree. Agent name cannot be "user", since it's reserved for end-user's input.

### `Optional`parentAgent[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LoopAgent.html#parentagent)

parentAgent?: [BaseAgent](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseAgent.html)

The parent agent of this agent.

Note that an agent can ONLY be added as sub-agent once.

If you want to add one agent twice as sub-agent, consider to create two agent instances with identical config, but with different name and add them to the agent tree.

The parent agent is the agent that created this agent.

### `Readonly`rootAgent[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LoopAgent.html#rootagent)

rootAgent: [BaseAgent](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseAgent.html)

Root agent of this agent.

### `Readonly`subAgents[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LoopAgent.html#subagents)

subAgents: [BaseAgent](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseAgent.html)
\[\]

The sub-agents of this agent.

# EventActions | ADK for TypeScript: API Reference

*   [EventActions](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/EventActions.html)
    

Interface EventActions
======================

Represents the actions attached to an event.

interface EventActions {  
    [artifactDelta](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/EventActions.html#artifactdelta)
: { \[key: string\]: number };  
    [escalate](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/EventActions.html#escalate)
?: boolean;  
    [requestedAuthConfigs](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/EventActions.html#requestedauthconfigs)
: { \[key: string\]: any };  
    [requestedToolConfirmations](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/EventActions.html#requestedtoolconfirmations)
: { \[key: string\]: [ToolConfirmation](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/ToolConfirmation.html)
 };  
    [skipSummarization](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/EventActions.html#skipsummarization)
?: boolean;  
    [stateDelta](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/EventActions.html#statedelta)
: { \[key: string\]: unknown };  
    [transferToAgent](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/EventActions.html#transfertoagent)
?: string;  
}

Properties
----------

### artifactDelta[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/EventActions.html#artifactdelta)

artifactDelta: { \[key: string\]: number }

Indicates that the event is updating an artifact. key is the filename, value is the version.

### `Optional`escalate[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/EventActions.html#escalate)

escalate?: boolean

The agent is escalating to a higher level agent.

### requestedAuthConfigs[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/EventActions.html#requestedauthconfigs)

requestedAuthConfigs: { \[key: string\]: any }

Authentication configurations requested by tool responses.

This field will only be set by a tool response event indicating tool request auth credential.

*   Keys: The function call id. Since one function response event could contain multiple function responses that correspond to multiple function calls. Each function call could request different auth configs. This id is used to identify the function call.
*   Values: The requested auth config.

### requestedToolConfirmations[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/EventActions.html#requestedtoolconfirmations)

requestedToolConfirmations: { \[key: string\]: [ToolConfirmation](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/ToolConfirmation.html)
 }

A dict of tool confirmation requested by this event, keyed by the function call id.

### `Optional`skipSummarization[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/EventActions.html#skipsummarization)

skipSummarization?: boolean

If true, it won't call model to summarize function response. Only used for function\_response event.

### stateDelta[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/EventActions.html#statedelta)

stateDelta: { \[key: string\]: unknown }

Indicates that the event is updating the state with the given delta.

### `Optional`transferToAgent[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/EventActions.html#transfertoagent)

transferToAgent?: string

If set, the event transfers to the specified agent.

Properties

[artifactDelta](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/EventActions.html#artifactdelta)
[escalate](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/EventActions.html#escalate)
[requestedAuthConfigs](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/EventActions.html#requestedauthconfigs)
[requestedToolConfirmations](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/EventActions.html#requestedtoolconfirmations)
[skipSummarization](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/EventActions.html#skipsummarization)
[stateDelta](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/EventActions.html#statedelta)
[transferToAgent](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/EventActions.html#transfertoagent)

# MCPTool | ADK for TypeScript: API Reference

*   [MCPTool](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/MCPTool.html)
    

Class MCPTool
=============

Represents a tool exposed via the Model Context Protocol (MCP).

This class acts as a wrapper around a tool definition received from an MCP server. It translates the MCP tool's schema into a format compatible with the Gemini AI platform (FunctionDeclaration) and handles the remote execution of the tool by communicating with the MCP server through an [MCPSessionManager](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/MCPSessionManager.html)
.

When an LLM decides to call this tool, the `runAsync` method will be invoked, which in turn establishes an MCP session, sends a `callTool` request with the provided arguments, and returns the result from the remote tool.

#### Hierarchy ([View Summary](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/hierarchy.html#MCPTool)
)

*   [BaseTool](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseTool.html)
    *   MCPTool

Constructors
------------

### constructor[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/MCPTool.html#constructor)

*   new MCPTool(  
        mcpTool: { \[key: string\]: unknown },  
        mcpSessionManager: [MCPSessionManager](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/MCPSessionManager.html)
    ,  
    ): [MCPTool](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/MCPTool.html)
    [](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/MCPTool.html#constructormcptool)
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   mcpTool: { \[key: string\]: unknown }
    *   mcpSessionManager: [MCPSessionManager](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/MCPSessionManager.html)
        
    
    #### Returns [MCPTool](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/MCPTool.html)

Accessors
---------

### apiVariant[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/MCPTool.html#apivariant)

*   get apiVariant(): GoogleLLMVariant
    
    The Google API LLM variant to use.
    
    #### Returns GoogleLLMVariant
    

Methods
-------

### \_getDeclaration[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/MCPTool.html#_getdeclaration)

*   \_getDeclaration(): FunctionDeclaration[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/MCPTool.html#_getdeclaration-1)
    
    Gets the OpenAPI specification of this tool in the form of a FunctionDeclaration.
    
    NOTE
    
    *   Required if subclass uses the default implementation of `processLlmRequest` to add function declaration to LLM request.
    *   Otherwise, can be skipped, e.g. for a built-in GoogleSearch tool for Gemini.
    
    #### Returns FunctionDeclaration
    
    The FunctionDeclaration of this tool, or undefined if it doesn't need to be added to LlmRequest.config.
    

### processLlmRequest[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/MCPTool.html#processllmrequest)

*   processLlmRequest(request: [ToolProcessLlmRequest](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/ToolProcessLlmRequest.html)
    ): Promise<void\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/MCPTool.html#processllmrequest-1)
    
    Processes the outgoing LLM request for this tool.
    
    Use cases:
    
    *   Most common use case is adding this tool to the LLM request.
    *   Some tools may just preprocess the LLM request before it's sent out.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   request: [ToolProcessLlmRequest](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/ToolProcessLlmRequest.html)
        
        The request to process the LLM request.
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<void\>
    

### runAsync[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/MCPTool.html#runasync)

*   runAsync(request: [RunAsyncToolRequest](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/RunAsyncToolRequest.html)
    ): Promise<unknown\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/MCPTool.html#runasync-1)
    
    Runs the tool with the given arguments and context.
    
    NOTE
    
    *   Required if this tool needs to run at the client side.
    *   Otherwise, can be skipped, e.g. for a built-in GoogleSearch tool for Gemini.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   request: [RunAsyncToolRequest](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/RunAsyncToolRequest.html)
        
        The request to run the tool.
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<unknown\>
    
    A promise that resolves to the tool response.

# ParallelAgent | ADK for TypeScript: API Reference

*   [ParallelAgent](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/ParallelAgent.html)
    

Class ParallelAgent
===================

A shell agent that run its sub-agents in parallel in isolated manner.

This approach is beneficial for scenarios requiring multiple perspectives or attempts on a single task, such as:

*   Running different algorithms simultaneously.
*   Generating multiple responses for review by a subsequent evaluation agent.

#### Hierarchy ([View Summary](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/hierarchy.html#ParallelAgent)
)

*   [BaseAgent](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseAgent.html)
    *   ParallelAgent

Constructors
------------

### constructor[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/ParallelAgent.html#constructor)

*   new ParallelAgent(config: BaseAgentConfig): [ParallelAgent](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/ParallelAgent.html)
    [](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/ParallelAgent.html#constructorparallelagent)
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   config: BaseAgentConfig
    
    #### Returns [ParallelAgent](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/ParallelAgent.html)
    

Properties
----------

### `Readonly`afterAgentCallback[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/ParallelAgent.html#afteragentcallback)

afterAgentCallback: SingleAgentCallback\[\]

Callback or list of callbacks to be invoked after the agent run.

When a list of callbacks is provided, the callbacks will be called in the order they are listed until a callback does not return undefined.

#### Param: callbackContext:[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/ParallelAgent.html#param-callbackcontext)

MUST be named 'callbackContext' (enforced).

#### Returns[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/ParallelAgent.html#returns)

Content: The content to return to the user. When the content is present, the provided content will be used as agent response and appended to event history as agent response.

### `Readonly`beforeAgentCallback[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/ParallelAgent.html#beforeagentcallback)

beforeAgentCallback: SingleAgentCallback\[\]

Callback or list of callbacks to be invoked before the agent run.

When a list of callbacks is provided, the callbacks will be called in the order they are listed until a callback does not return undefined.

#### Param: callbackContext:[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/ParallelAgent.html#param-callbackcontext-1)

MUST be named 'callbackContext' (enforced).

#### Returns[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/ParallelAgent.html#returns-1)

Content: The content to return to the user. When the content is present, the agent run will be skipped and the provided content will be returned to user.

### `Optional` `Readonly`description[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/ParallelAgent.html#description)

description?: string

Description about the agent's capability.

The model uses this to determine whether to delegate control to the agent. One-line description is enough and preferred.

### `Readonly`name[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/ParallelAgent.html#name)

name: string

The agent's name. Agent name must be a JS identifier and unique within the agent tree. Agent name cannot be "user", since it's reserved for end-user's input.

### `Optional`parentAgent[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/ParallelAgent.html#parentagent)

parentAgent?: [BaseAgent](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseAgent.html)

The parent agent of this agent.

Note that an agent can ONLY be added as sub-agent once.

If you want to add one agent twice as sub-agent, consider to create two agent instances with identical config, but with different name and add them to the agent tree.

The parent agent is the agent that created this agent.

### `Readonly`rootAgent[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/ParallelAgent.html#rootagent)

rootAgent: [BaseAgent](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseAgent.html)

Root agent of this agent.

### `Readonly`subAgents[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/ParallelAgent.html#subagents)

subAgents: [BaseAgent](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseAgent.html)
\[\]

The sub-agents of this agent.

# SequentialAgent | ADK for TypeScript: API Reference

*   [SequentialAgent](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/SequentialAgent.html)
    

Class SequentialAgent
=====================

A shell agent that runs its sub-agents in a sequential order.

#### Hierarchy ([View Summary](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/hierarchy.html#SequentialAgent)
)

*   [BaseAgent](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseAgent.html)
    *   SequentialAgent

afterAgentCallback: SingleAgentCallback\[\]

When a list of callbacks is provided, the callbacks will be called in the order they are listed until a callback does not return undefined.

Content: The content to return to the user. When the content is present, the provided content will be used as agent response and appended to event history as agent response.

*   runLive(parentContext: [InvocationContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/InvocationContext.html)
    ): AsyncGenerator<[Event](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Event.html)
    , void, void\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/SequentialAgent.html#runlive-1)
    
    Entry method to run an agent via video/audio-based conversation.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   parentContext: [InvocationContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/InvocationContext.html)
        
        The invocation context of the parent agent.
        
    
    #### Returns AsyncGenerator<[Event](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Event.html)
    , void, void\>
    
    An AsyncGenerator that yields the events generated by the agent.
    
    #### Yields[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/SequentialAgent.html#yields-2)
    
    The events generated by the agent.
    

### `Protected`runLiveImpl[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/SequentialAgent.html#runliveimpl)

*   runLiveImpl(context: [InvocationContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/InvocationContext.html)
    ): AsyncGenerator<[Event](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Event.html)
    , void, void\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/SequentialAgent.html#runliveimpl-1)
    
    Implementation for live SequentialAgent.
    
    Compared to the non-live case, live agents process a continuous stream of audio or video, so there is no way to tell if it's finished and should pass to the next agent or not. So we introduce a task\_completed() function so the model can call this function to signal that it's finished the task and we can move on to the next agent.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   context: [InvocationContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/InvocationContext.html)
        
    
    #### Returns AsyncGenerator<[Event](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Event.html)
    , void, void\>

# LlmRequest | ADK for TypeScript: API Reference

*   [LlmRequest](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmRequest.html)
    

Interface LlmRequest
====================

LLM request class that allows passing in tools, output schema and system instructions to the model.

interface LlmRequest {  
    [config](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmRequest.html#config)
?: GenerateContentConfig;  
    [contents](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmRequest.html#contents)
: Content\[\];  
    [liveConnectConfig](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmRequest.html#liveconnectconfig)
: LiveConnectConfig;  
    [model](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmRequest.html#model)
?: string;  
    [toolsDict](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmRequest.html#toolsdict)
: { \[key: string\]: [BaseTool](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseTool.html)
 };  
}

Properties
----------

### `Optional`config[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmRequest.html#config)

config?: GenerateContentConfig

Additional config for the generate content request. Tools in generateContentConfig should not be set directly; use appendTools.

### contents[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmRequest.html#contents)

contents: Content\[\]

The contents to send to the model.

### liveConnectConfig[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmRequest.html#liveconnectconfig)

liveConnectConfig: LiveConnectConfig

### `Optional`model[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmRequest.html#model)

model?: string

The model name.

### toolsDict[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmRequest.html#toolsdict)

toolsDict: { \[key: string\]: [BaseTool](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseTool.html)
 }

The tools dictionary. Excluded from JSON serialization.

# StreamableHTTPConnectionParams | ADK for TypeScript: API Reference

*   [StreamableHTTPConnectionParams](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/StreamableHTTPConnectionParams.html)
    

Interface StreamableHTTPConnectionParams
========================================

Defines the parameters for establishing a connection to an MCP server over HTTP using Server-Sent Events (SSE) for streaming.

Usage: const connectionParams: StreamableHTTPConnectionParams = { type: 'StreamableHTTPConnectionParams', url: '[http://localhost:8788/mcp](http://localhost:8788/mcp)
' };

interface StreamableHTTPConnectionParams {  
    [header](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/StreamableHTTPConnectionParams.html#header)
?: Record<string, unknown\>;  
    [sseReadTimeout](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/StreamableHTTPConnectionParams.html#ssereadtimeout)
?: Number;  
    [terminateOnClose](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/StreamableHTTPConnectionParams.html#terminateonclose)
?: boolean;  
    [timeout](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/StreamableHTTPConnectionParams.html#timeout)
?: Number;  
    [type](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/StreamableHTTPConnectionParams.html#type)
: "StreamableHTTPConnectionParams";  
    [url](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/StreamableHTTPConnectionParams.html#url)
: string;  
}

Properties
----------

header?: Record<string, unknown\>

### `Optional`sseReadTimeout[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/StreamableHTTPConnectionParams.html#ssereadtimeout)

sseReadTimeout?: Number

### `Optional`terminateOnClose[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/StreamableHTTPConnectionParams.html#terminateonclose)

terminateOnClose?: boolean

### `Optional`timeout[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/StreamableHTTPConnectionParams.html#timeout)

timeout?: Number

### type[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/StreamableHTTPConnectionParams.html#type)

type: "StreamableHTTPConnectionParams"

### url[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/StreamableHTTPConnectionParams.html#url)

url: string

# LlmResponse | ADK for TypeScript: API Reference

*   [LlmResponse](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html)
    

Interface LlmResponse
=====================

LLM response class that provides the first candidate response from the model if available. Otherwise, returns error code and message.

interface LlmResponse {  
    [content](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html#content)
?: Content;  
    [customMetadata](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html#custommetadata)
?: { \[key: string\]: any };  
    [errorCode](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html#errorcode)
?: string;  
    [errorMessage](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html#errormessage)
?: string;  
    [finishReason](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html#finishreason)
?: FinishReason;  
    [groundingMetadata](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html#groundingmetadata)
?: GroundingMetadata;  
    [inputTranscription](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html#inputtranscription)
?: Transcription;  
    [interrupted](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html#interrupted)
?: boolean;  
    [liveSessionResumptionUpdate](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html#livesessionresumptionupdate)
?: LiveServerSessionResumptionUpdate;  
    [outputTranscription](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html#outputtranscription)
?: Transcription;  
    [partial](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html#partial)
?: boolean;  
    [turnComplete](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html#turncomplete)
?: boolean;  
    [usageMetadata](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html#usagemetadata)
?: GenerateContentResponseUsageMetadata;  
}

#### Hierarchy ([View Summary](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/hierarchy.html#LlmResponse)
)

*   LlmResponse
    *   [Event](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Event.html)
        

Properties
----------

### `Optional`content[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html#content)

content?: Content

The content of the response.

### `Optional`customMetadata[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html#custommetadata)

customMetadata?: { \[key: string\]: any }

The custom metadata of the LlmResponse. An optional key-value pair to label an LlmResponse. NOTE: the entire object must be JSON serializable.

### `Optional`errorCode[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html#errorcode)

errorCode?: string

Error code if the response is an error. Code varies by model.

### `Optional`errorMessage[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html#errormessage)

errorMessage?: string

Error message if the response is an error.

### `Optional`finishReason[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html#finishreason)

finishReason?: FinishReason

The finish reason of the response.

### `Optional`groundingMetadata[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html#groundingmetadata)

groundingMetadata?: GroundingMetadata

The grounding metadata of the response.

### `Optional`inputTranscription[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html#inputtranscription)

inputTranscription?: Transcription

Audio transcription of user input.

### `Optional`interrupted[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html#interrupted)

interrupted?: boolean

Flag indicating that LLM was interrupted when generating the content. Usually it's due to user interruption during a bidi streaming.

### `Optional`liveSessionResumptionUpdate[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html#livesessionresumptionupdate)

liveSessionResumptionUpdate?: LiveServerSessionResumptionUpdate

The session resumption update of the LlmResponse

### `Optional`outputTranscription[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html#outputtranscription)

outputTranscription?: Transcription

Audio transcription of model output.

### `Optional`partial[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html#partial)

partial?: boolean

Indicates whether the text content is part of a unfinished text stream. Only used for streaming mode and when the content is plain text.

### `Optional`turnComplete[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html#turncomplete)

turnComplete?: boolean

Indicates whether the response from the model is complete. Only used for streaming mode.

### `Optional`usageMetadata[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html#usagemetadata)

usageMetadata?: GenerateContentResponseUsageMetadata

The usage metadata of the LlmResponse.

# RunConfig | ADK for TypeScript: API Reference

*   [RunConfig](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/RunConfig.html)
    

Interface RunConfig
===================

Configs for runtime behavior of agents.

interface RunConfig {  
    [enableAffectiveDialog](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/RunConfig.html#enableaffectivedialog)
?: boolean;  
    [inputAudioTranscription](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/RunConfig.html#inputaudiotranscription)
?: AudioTranscriptionConfig;  
    [maxLlmCalls](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/RunConfig.html#maxllmcalls)
?: number;  
    [outputAudioTranscription](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/RunConfig.html#outputaudiotranscription)
?: AudioTranscriptionConfig;  
    [proactivity](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/RunConfig.html#proactivity)
?: ProactivityConfig;  
    [realtimeInputConfig](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/RunConfig.html#realtimeinputconfig)
?: RealtimeInputConfig;  
    [responseModalities](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/RunConfig.html#responsemodalities)
?: Modality\[\];  
    [saveInputBlobsAsArtifacts](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/RunConfig.html#saveinputblobsasartifacts)
?: boolean;  
    [speechConfig](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/RunConfig.html#speechconfig)
?: SpeechConfig;  
    [streamingMode](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/RunConfig.html#streamingmode)
?: [StreamingMode](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/enums/StreamingMode.html)
;  
    [supportCfc](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/RunConfig.html#supportcfc)
?: boolean;  
}

Properties
----------

### `Optional`enableAffectiveDialog[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/RunConfig.html#enableaffectivedialog)

enableAffectiveDialog?: boolean

If enabled, the model will detect emotions and adapt its responses accordingly.

### `Optional`inputAudioTranscription[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/RunConfig.html#inputaudiotranscription)

inputAudioTranscription?: AudioTranscriptionConfig

Input transcription for live agents with audio input from user.

### `Optional`maxLlmCalls[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/RunConfig.html#maxllmcalls)

maxLlmCalls?: number

A limit on the total number of llm calls for a given run.

Valid Values:

*   More than 0 and less than sys.maxsize: The bound on the number of llm calls is enforced, if the value is set in this range.
*   Less than or equal to 0: This allows for unbounded number of llm calls.

### `Optional`outputAudioTranscription[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/RunConfig.html#outputaudiotranscription)

outputAudioTranscription?: AudioTranscriptionConfig

Output audio transcription config.

### `Optional`proactivity[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/RunConfig.html#proactivity)

proactivity?: ProactivityConfig

Configures the proactivity of the model. This allows the model to respond proactively to the input and to ignore irrelevant input.

### `Optional`realtimeInputConfig[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/RunConfig.html#realtimeinputconfig)

realtimeInputConfig?: RealtimeInputConfig

Realtime input config for live agents with audio input from user.

### `Optional`responseModalities[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/RunConfig.html#responsemodalities)

responseModalities?: Modality\[\]

The output modalities. If not set, it's default to AUDIO.

### `Optional`saveInputBlobsAsArtifacts[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/RunConfig.html#saveinputblobsasartifacts)

saveInputBlobsAsArtifacts?: boolean

Whether or not to save the input blobs as artifacts.

### `Optional`speechConfig[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/RunConfig.html#speechconfig)

speechConfig?: SpeechConfig

Speech configuration for the live agent.

### `Optional`streamingMode[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/RunConfig.html#streamingmode)

streamingMode?: [StreamingMode](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/enums/StreamingMode.html)

Streaming mode, None or StreamingMode.SSE or StreamingMode.BIDI.

### `Optional`supportCfc[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/RunConfig.html#supportcfc)

supportCfc?: boolean

Whether to support CFC (Compositional Function Calling). Only applicable for StreamingMode.SSE. If it's true. the LIVE API will be invoked. Since only LIVE API supports CFC

WARNING: This feature is **experimental** and its API or behavior may change in future releases.

Properties

[enableAffectiveDialog](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/RunConfig.html#enableaffectivedialog)
[inputAudioTranscription](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/RunConfig.html#inputaudiotranscription)
[maxLlmCalls](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/RunConfig.html#maxllmcalls)

[proactivity](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/RunConfig.html#proactivity)
[realtimeInputConfig](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/RunConfig.html#realtimeinputconfig)

# Session | ADK for TypeScript: API Reference

*   [Session](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Session.html)
    

Interface Session
=================

Represents a session in a conversation between agents and users.

interface Session {  
    [appName](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Session.html#appname)
: string;  
    [events](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Session.html#events)
: [Event](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Event.html)
\[\];  
    [id](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Session.html#id)
: string;  
    [lastUpdateTime](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Session.html#lastupdatetime)
: number;  
    [state](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Session.html#state)
: Record<string, unknown\>;  
    [userId](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Session.html#userid)
: string;  
}

Properties
----------

### appName[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Session.html#appname)

events: [Event](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Event.html)
\[\]

id: string

The unique identifier of the session.

lastUpdateTime: number

The last update time of the session.

### state[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Session.html#state)

state: Record<string, unknown\>

# maybeSetOtelProviders | ADK for TypeScript: API Reference

*   [maybeSetOtelProviders](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/functions/maybeSetOtelProviders.html)
    

Function maybeSetOtelProviders
==============================

*   maybeSetOtelProviders(  
        otelHooksToSetup?: [OTelHooks](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/OTelHooks.html)
    \[\],  
        otelResource?: Resource,  
    ): void[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/functions/maybeSetOtelProviders.html#maybesetotelproviders)
    
    `Experimental`
    
    Sets up OTel providers if hooks for a given telemetry type were passed.
    
    Additionally adds generic OTLP exporters based on following env variables: OTEL\_EXPORTER\_OTLP\_ENDPOINT OTEL\_EXPORTER\_OTLP\_TRACES\_ENDPOINT OTEL\_EXPORTER\_OTLP\_METRICS\_ENDPOINT OTEL\_EXPORTER\_OTLP\_LOGS\_ENDPOINT See [https://opentelemetry.io/docs/languages/sdk-configuration/otlp-exporter/](https://opentelemetry.io/docs/languages/sdk-configuration/otlp-exporter/)
     for how they are used.
    
    If a provider for a specific telemetry type was already globally set - this function will not override it or register more exporters.
    
    (Experimental, subject to change)
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   otelHooksToSetup: [OTelHooks](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/OTelHooks.html)
        \[\] = \[\]
        
        per-telemetry-type processors and readers to be added to OTel providers. If no hooks for a specific telemetry type are passed - provider will not be set.
        
    *   `Optional`otelResource: Resource
        
        OTel resource to use in providers. If empty - default OTel resource detection will be used.
        
    
    #### Returns void

---

    ): Promise<number\[\]\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/InMemoryArtifactService.html#listversions-1)

        The request to list versions.

### loadArtifact[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/InMemoryArtifactService.html#loadartifact)

*   loadArtifact(request: [LoadArtifactRequest](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LoadArtifactRequest.html)
    ): Promise<Part | undefined\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/InMemoryArtifactService.html#loadartifact-1)
    
    Gets an artifact from the artifact service storage.
    
    The artifact is a file identified by the app name, user ID, session ID, and filename.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   request: [LoadArtifactRequest](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LoadArtifactRequest.html)
        
        The request to load an artifact.
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<Part | undefined\>
    
    A promise that resolves to the artifact or undefined if not found.
    

### saveArtifact[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/InMemoryArtifactService.html#saveartifact)

*   saveArtifact(request: [SaveArtifactRequest](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/SaveArtifactRequest.html)
    ): Promise<number\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/InMemoryArtifactService.html#saveartifact-1)
    
    Saves an artifact to the artifact service storage.
    
    The artifact is a file identified by the app name, user ID, session ID, and filename. After saving the artifact, a revision ID is returned to identify the artifact version.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   request: [SaveArtifactRequest](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/SaveArtifactRequest.html)
        
        The request to save an artifact.
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<number\>
    
    A promise that resolves to The revision ID. The first version of the artifact has a revision ID of 0. This is incremented by 1 after each successful save.

# BaseMemoryService | ADK for TypeScript: API Reference

*   [BaseMemoryService](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/BaseMemoryService.html)
    

Interface BaseMemoryService
===========================

Base interface for memory services.

The service provides functionalities to ingest sessions into memory so that the memory can be used for user queries.

interface BaseMemoryService {  
    [addSessionToMemory](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/BaseMemoryService.html#addsessiontomemory-1)
(session: [Session](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Session.html)
): Promise<void\>;  
    [searchMemory](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/BaseMemoryService.html#searchmemory-1)
(request: [SearchMemoryRequest](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/SearchMemoryRequest.html)
): Promise<[SearchMemoryResponse](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/SearchMemoryResponse.html)
\>;  
}

# Event | ADK for TypeScript: API Reference

*   [Event](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Event.html)
    

Interface Event
===============

Represents an event in a conversation between agents and users.

It is used to store the content of the conversation, as well as the actions taken by the agents like function calls, etc.

interface Event {  
    [actions](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Event.html#actions)
: [EventActions](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/EventActions.html)
;  
    [author](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Event.html#author)
?: string;  
    [branch](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Event.html#branch)
?: string;  
    [content](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Event.html#content)
?: Content;  
    [customMetadata](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Event.html#custommetadata)
?: { \[key: string\]: any };  
    [errorCode](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Event.html#errorcode)
?: string;  
    [errorMessage](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Event.html#errormessage)
?: string;  
    [finishReason](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Event.html#finishreason)
?: FinishReason;  
    [groundingMetadata](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Event.html#groundingmetadata)
?: GroundingMetadata;  
    [id](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Event.html#id)
: string;  
    [inputTranscription](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Event.html#inputtranscription)
?: Transcription;

?: boolean;

: string;

?: LiveServerSessionResumptionUpdate;  
    [longRunningToolIds](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Event.html#longrunningtoolids)
?: string\[\];  
    [outputTranscription](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Event.html#outputtranscription)
?: Transcription;  
    [partial](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Event.html#partial)
?: boolean;

: number;

?: boolean;

?: GenerateContentResponseUsageMetadata;  
}

#### Hierarchy ([View Summary](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/hierarchy.html#Event)
)

*   [LlmResponse](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html)
    *   Event

Properties
----------

### actions[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Event.html#actions)

actions: [EventActions](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/EventActions.html)

The actions taken by the agent.

### `Optional`author[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Event.html#author)

author?: string

"user" or the name of the agent, indicating who appended the event to the session.

### `Optional`branch[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Event.html#branch)

branch?: string

The branch of the event. The format is like agent\_1.agent\_2.agent\_3, where agent\_1 is the parent of agent\_2, and agent\_2 is the parent of agent\_3.

Branch is used when multiple sub-agent shouldn't see their peer agents' conversation history.

### `Optional`content[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Event.html#content)

content?: Content

The content of the response.

### `Optional`customMetadata[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Event.html#custommetadata)

customMetadata?: { \[key: string\]: any }

The custom metadata of the LlmResponse. An optional key-value pair to label an LlmResponse. NOTE: the entire object must be JSON serializable.

### `Optional`errorCode[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Event.html#errorcode)

errorCode?: string

Error code if the response is an error. Code varies by model.

### `Optional`errorMessage[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Event.html#errormessage)

errorMessage?: string

Error message if the response is an error.

### `Optional`finishReason[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Event.html#finishreason)

finishReason?: FinishReason

The finish reason of the response.

### `Optional`groundingMetadata[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Event.html#groundingmetadata)

groundingMetadata?: GroundingMetadata

The grounding metadata of the response.

### id[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Event.html#id)

id: string

The unique identifier of the event. Do not assign the ID. It will be assigned by the session.

### `Optional`inputTranscription[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Event.html#inputtranscription)

inputTranscription?: Transcription

Audio transcription of user input.

### `Optional`interrupted[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Event.html#interrupted)

interrupted?: boolean

Flag indicating that LLM was interrupted when generating the content. Usually it's due to user interruption during a bidi streaming.

### invocationId[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Event.html#invocationid)

invocationId: string

The invocation ID of the event. Should be non-empty before appending to a session.

### `Optional`liveSessionResumptionUpdate[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Event.html#livesessionresumptionupdate)

liveSessionResumptionUpdate?: LiveServerSessionResumptionUpdate

The session resumption update of the LlmResponse

### `Optional`longRunningToolIds[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Event.html#longrunningtoolids)

longRunningToolIds?: string\[\]

Set of ids of the long running function calls. Agent client will know from this field about which function call is long running. Only valid for function call event

### `Optional`outputTranscription[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Event.html#outputtranscription)

outputTranscription?: Transcription

Audio transcription of model output.

### `Optional`partial[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Event.html#partial)

partial?: boolean

Indicates whether the text content is part of a unfinished text stream. Only used for streaming mode and when the content is plain text.

### timestamp[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Event.html#timestamp)

timestamp: number

The timestamp of the event.

### `Optional`turnComplete[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Event.html#turncomplete)

turnComplete?: boolean

Indicates whether the response from the model is complete. Only used for streaming mode.

### `Optional`usageMetadata[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Event.html#usagemetadata)

usageMetadata?: GenerateContentResponseUsageMetadata

The usage metadata of the LlmResponse.

# MCPToolset | ADK for TypeScript: API Reference

*   [MCPToolset](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/MCPToolset.html)
    

Class MCPToolset
================

A toolset that dynamically discovers and provides tools from a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server.

This class connects to an MCP server, retrieves the list of available tools, and wraps each of them in an [MCPTool](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/MCPTool.html)
 instance. This allows the agent to seamlessly use tools from an external MCP-compliant service.

The toolset can be configured with a filter to selectively expose a subset of the tools provided by the MCP server.

Usage: import { MCPToolset } from '@google/adk'; import { StreamableHTTPConnectionParamsSchema } from '@google/adk';

const connectionParams = StreamableHTTPConnectionParamsSchema.parse({ type: "StreamableHTTPConnectionParams", url: "[http://localhost:8788/mcp](http://localhost:8788/mcp)
" });

const mcpToolset = new MCPToolset(connectionParams); const tools = await mcpToolset.getTools();

#### Hierarchy ([View Summary](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/hierarchy.html#MCPToolset)
)

*   [BaseToolset](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseToolset.html)
    *   MCPToolset

Constructors
------------

### constructor[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/MCPToolset.html#constructor)

*   new MCPToolset(  
        connectionParams: [MCPConnectionParams](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/types/MCPConnectionParams.html)
    ,  
        toolFilter?: string\[\] | ToolPredicate,  
    ): [MCPToolset](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/MCPToolset.html)
    [](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/MCPToolset.html#constructormcptoolset)
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   connectionParams: [MCPConnectionParams](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/types/MCPConnectionParams.html)
        
    *   toolFilter: string\[\] | ToolPredicate = \[\]
    
    #### Returns [MCPToolset](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/MCPToolset.html)
    

Properties
----------

### `Readonly`toolFilter[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/MCPToolset.html#toolfilter)

toolFilter: string\[\] | ToolPredicate

Methods
-------

### close[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/MCPToolset.html#close)

*   close(): Promise<void\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/MCPToolset.html#close-1)
    
    Closes the toolset.
    
    NOTE: This method is invoked, for example, at the end of an agent server's lifecycle or when the toolset is no longer needed. Implementations should ensure that any open connections, files, or other managed resources are properly released to prevent leaks.
    
    #### Returns Promise<void\>
    
    A Promise that resolves when the toolset is closed.
    

### getTools[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/MCPToolset.html#gettools)

*   getTools(context?: ReadonlyContext): Promise<[BaseTool](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseTool.html)
    \[\]\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/MCPToolset.html#gettools-1)
    
    Returns the tools that should be exposed to LLM.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   `Optional`context: ReadonlyContext
        
        Context used to filter tools available to the agent. If not defined, all tools in the toolset are returned.
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<[BaseTool](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseTool.html)
    \[\]\>
    
    A Promise that resolves to the list of tools.
    

### `Protected`isToolSelected[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/MCPToolset.html#istoolselected)

*   isToolSelected(tool: [BaseTool](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseTool.html)
    , context: ReadonlyContext): boolean[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/MCPToolset.html#istoolselected-1)
    
    Returns whether the tool should be exposed to LLM.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   tool: [BaseTool](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseTool.html)
        
        The tool to check.
        
    *   context: ReadonlyContext
        
        Context used to filter tools available to the agent.
        
    
    #### Returns boolean
    
    Whether the tool should be exposed to LLM.
    

### processLlmRequest[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/MCPToolset.html#processllmrequest)

*   processLlmRequest(  
        toolContext: [ToolContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/ToolContext.html)
    ,  
        llmRequest: [LlmRequest](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmRequest.html)
    ,  
    ): Promise<void\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/MCPToolset.html#processllmrequest-1)
    
    Processes the outgoing LLM request for this toolset. This method will be called before each tool processes the llm request.
    
    Use cases:
    
    *   Instead of let each tool process the llm request, we can let the toolset process the llm request. e.g. ComputerUseToolset can add computer use tool to the llm request.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   toolContext: [ToolContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/ToolContext.html)
        
        The context of the tool.
        
    *   llmRequest: [LlmRequest](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmRequest.html)
        
        The outgoing LLM request, mutable this method.
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<void\>

# BasePlugin | ADK for TypeScript: API Reference

*   [BasePlugin](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BasePlugin.html)
    

Class BasePlugin`Abstract`
==========================

Base class for creating plugins.

Plugins provide a structured way to intercept and modify agent, tool, and LLM behaviors at critical execution points in a callback manner. While agent callbacks apply to a particular agent, plugins applies globally to all agents added in the runner. Plugins are best used for adding custom behaviors like logging, monitoring, caching, or modifying requests and responses at key stages.

A plugin can implement one or more methods of callbacks, but should not implement the same method of callback for multiple times.

Relation with [Agent callbacks](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/callbacks/)
:

**Execution Order** Similar to Agent callbacks, Plugins are executed in the order they are registered. However, Plugin and Agent Callbacks are executed sequentially, with Plugins takes precedence over agent callbacks. When the callback in a plugin returns a value, it will short circuit all remaining plugins and agent callbacks, causing all remaining plugins and agent callbacks to be skipped.

**Change Propagation** Plugins and agent callbacks can both modify the value of the input parameters, including agent input, tool input, and LLM request/response, etc. They work in the exactly same way. The modifications will be visible and passed to the next callback in the chain. For example, if a plugin modifies the tool input with before\_tool\_callback, the modified tool input will be passed to the before\_tool\_callback of the next plugin, and further passed to the agent callbacks if not short circuited.

To use a plugin, implement the desired callback methods and pass an instance of your custom plugin class to the ADK Runner.

Example: A simple plugin that logs every tool call.

    class ToolLoggerPlugin extends BasePlugin {  constructor() {    super('tool_logger');  }  override async beforeToolCallback(    {tool, toolArgs, toolContext}: {      tool: BaseTool,      toolArgs: Record<string, unknown>,      toolContext: ToolContext,    },  ): Promise<Record<string, unknown> | undefined> {    this.logger.info(      `[${this.name}] Calling tool '${tool.name}' with args:${JSON.stringify( toolArgs,      )}`,    );    return;  }  override async afterToolCallback(    {tool, toolArgs, toolContext, result}: {      tool: BaseTool,      toolArgs: Record<string, unknown>,      toolContext: ToolContext,      result: Record<string, unknown>,    },  ): Promise<Record<string, unknown> | undefined> {    this.logger.info(      `[${this.name}] Tool '${tool.name}' finished with result:${JSON.stringify( result,      )}`,    );    return;  }}// Add the plugin to ADK Runner// runner = new Runner({//   ...//   plugins: [new ToolLoggerPlugin(), new AgentPolicyPlugin()],// });
    

#### Hierarchy ([View Summary](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/hierarchy.html#BasePlugin)
)

*   BasePlugin
    *   [LoggingPlugin](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LoggingPlugin.html)
        
    *   [SecurityPlugin](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/SecurityPlugin.html)

Methods

### afterAgentCallback[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BasePlugin.html#afteragentcallback)

*   afterAgentCallback(  
        agent: { agent: [BaseAgent](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseAgent.html)
    ; callbackContext: [CallbackContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/CallbackContext.html)
     },  
    ): Promise<Content | undefined\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BasePlugin.html#afteragentcallback-1)
    
    Callback executed after an agent's primary logic has completed.
    
    This callback can be used to inspect, log, or modify the agent's final result before it is returned.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   agent: { agent: [BaseAgent](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseAgent.html)
        ; callbackContext: [CallbackContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/CallbackContext.html)
         }
        
        The agent that has just run.
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<Content | undefined\>
    
    An optional `Content` object. If a value is returned, it will replace the agent's original result. Returning `undefined` uses the original, unmodified result.
    

### afterModelCallback[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BasePlugin.html#aftermodelcallback)

*   afterModelCallback(  
        callbackContext: {  
            callbackContext: [CallbackContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/CallbackContext.html)
    ;  
            llmResponse: [LlmResponse](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html)
    ;  
        },  
    ): Promise<[LlmResponse](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html)
     | undefined\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BasePlugin.html#aftermodelcallback-1)
    
    Callback executed after a response is received from the model.
    
    This is the ideal place to log model responses, collect metrics on token usage, or perform post-processing on the raw `LlmResponse`.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   callbackContext: { callbackContext: [CallbackContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/CallbackContext.html)
        ; llmResponse: [LlmResponse](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html)
         }
        
        The context for the current agent call.
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<[LlmResponse](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html)
     | undefined\>
    
    An optional value. A non-`undefined` return may be used by the framework to modify or replace the response. Returning `undefined` allows the original response to be used.
    

### afterRunCallback[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BasePlugin.html#afterruncallback)

*   afterRunCallback(  
        invocationContext: { invocationContext: [InvocationContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/InvocationContext.html)
     },  
    ): Promise<void\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BasePlugin.html#afterruncallback-1)
    
    Callback executed after an ADK runner run has completed.
    
    This is the final callback in the ADK lifecycle, suitable for cleanup, final logging, or reporting tasks.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   invocationContext: { invocationContext: [InvocationContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/InvocationContext.html)
         }
        
        The context for the entire invocation.
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<void\>
    
    undefined
    

### afterToolCallback[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BasePlugin.html#aftertoolcallback)

*   afterToolCallback(  
        tool: {  
            result: Record<string, unknown\>;  
            tool: [BaseTool](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseTool.html)
    ;  
            toolArgs: Record<string, unknown\>;  
            toolContext: [ToolContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/ToolContext.html)
    ;  
        },  
    ): Promise<Record<string, unknown\> | undefined\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BasePlugin.html#aftertoolcallback-1)
    
    Callback executed after a tool has been called.
    
    This callback allows for inspecting, logging, or modifying the result returned by a tool.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   tool: {  
            result: Record<string, unknown\>;  
            tool: [BaseTool](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseTool.html)
        ;  
            toolArgs: Record<string, unknown\>;  
            toolContext: [ToolContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/ToolContext.html)
        ;  
        }
        
        The tool instance that has just been executed.
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<Record<string, unknown\> | undefined\>
    
    An optional dictionary. If a dictionary is returned, it will **replace** the original result from the tool. This allows for post-processing or altering tool outputs. Returning `undefined` uses the original, unmodified result.
    

### beforeAgentCallback[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BasePlugin.html#beforeagentcallback)

*   beforeAgentCallback(  
        agent: { agent: [BaseAgent](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseAgent.html)
    ; callbackContext: [CallbackContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/CallbackContext.html)
     },  
    ): Promise<Content | undefined\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BasePlugin.html#beforeagentcallback-1)
    
    Callback executed before an agent's primary logic is invoked.
    
    This callback can be used for logging, setup, or to short-circuit the agent's execution by returning a value.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   agent: { agent: [BaseAgent](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseAgent.html)
        ; callbackContext: [CallbackContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/CallbackContext.html)
         }
        
        The agent that is about to run.
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<Content | undefined\>
    
    An optional `Content` object. If a value is returned, it will bypass the agent's callbacks and its execution, and return this value directly. Returning `undefined` allows the agent to proceed normally.
    

### beforeModelCallback[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BasePlugin.html#beforemodelcallback)

*   beforeModelCallback(  
        callbackContext: {  
            callbackContext: [CallbackContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/CallbackContext.html)
    ;  
            llmRequest: [LlmRequest](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmRequest.html)
    ;  
        },  
    ): Promise<[LlmResponse](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html)
     | undefined\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BasePlugin.html#beforemodelcallback-1)
    
    Callback executed before a request is sent to the model.
    
    This provides an opportunity to inspect, log, or modify the `LlmRequest` object. It can also be used to implement caching by returning a cached `LlmResponse`, which would skip the actual model call.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   callbackContext: { callbackContext: [CallbackContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/CallbackContext.html)
        ; llmRequest: [LlmRequest](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmRequest.html)
         }
        
        The context for the current agent call.
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<[LlmResponse](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html)
     | undefined\>
    
    An optional value. The interpretation of a non-`undefined` trigger an early exit and returns the response immediately. Returning `undefined` allows the LLM request to proceed normally.
    

### beforeRunCallback[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BasePlugin.html#beforeruncallback)

*   beforeRunCallback(  
        invocationContext: { invocationContext: [InvocationContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/InvocationContext.html)
     },  
    ): Promise<Content | undefined\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BasePlugin.html#beforeruncallback-1)
    
    Callback executed before the ADK runner runs.
    
    This is the first callback to be called in the lifecycle, ideal for global setup or initialization tasks.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   invocationContext: { invocationContext: [InvocationContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/InvocationContext.html)
         }
        
        The context for the entire invocation, containing session information, the root agent, etc.
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<Content | undefined\>
    
    An optional `Event` to be returned to the ADK. Returning a value to halt execution of the runner and ends the runner with that event. Return `undefined` to proceed normally.
    

### beforeToolCallback[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BasePlugin.html#beforetoolcallback)

*   beforeToolCallback(  
        tool: {

    ): Promise<Record<string, unknown\> | undefined\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BasePlugin.html#beforetoolcallback-1)

    This callback is useful for logging tool usage, input validation, or modifying the arguments before they are passed to the tool.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   tool: { tool: [BaseTool](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseTool.html)
        ; toolArgs: Record<string, unknown\>; toolContext: [ToolContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/ToolContext.html)
         }
        
        The tool instance that is about to be executed.
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<Record<string, unknown\> | undefined\>
    
    An optional dictionary. If a dictionary is returned, it will stop the tool execution and return this response immediately. Returning `undefined` uses the original, unmodified arguments.

     | undefined\>
    
    An optional value. A non-`undefined` return may be used by the framework to modify or replace the response. Returning `undefined` allows the original response to be used.
    

### onModelErrorCallback[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BasePlugin.html#onmodelerrorcallback)

*   onModelErrorCallback(  
        callbackContext: {  
            callbackContext: [CallbackContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/CallbackContext.html)
    ;  
            error: Error;  
            llmRequest: [LlmRequest](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmRequest.html)
    ;  
        },  
    ): Promise<[LlmResponse](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html)
     | undefined\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BasePlugin.html#onmodelerrorcallback-1)
    
    Callback executed when a model call encounters an error.
    
    This callback provides an opportunity to handle model errors gracefully, potentially providing alternative responses or recovery mechanisms.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   callbackContext: { callbackContext: [CallbackContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/CallbackContext.html)
        ; error: Error; llmRequest: [LlmRequest](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmRequest.html)
         }
        
        The context for the current agent call.
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<[LlmResponse](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html)
     | undefined\>
    
    An optional LlmResponse. If an LlmResponse is returned, it will be used instead of propagating the error. Returning `undefined` allows the original error to be raised.
    

### onToolErrorCallback[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BasePlugin.html#ontoolerrorcallback)

*   onToolErrorCallback(  
        tool: {  
            error: Error;

    ;  
        },  
    ): Promise<Record<string, unknown\> | undefined\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BasePlugin.html#ontoolerrorcallback-1)

    This callback provides an opportunity to handle tool errors gracefully, potentially providing alternative responses or recovery mechanisms.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   tool: {  
            error: Error;  
            tool: [BaseTool](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseTool.html)
        ;  
            toolArgs: Record<string, unknown\>;  
            toolContext: [ToolContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/ToolContext.html)
        ;  
        }
        
        The tool instance that encountered an error.
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<Record<string, unknown\> | undefined\>
    
    An optional dictionary. If a dictionary is returned, it will be used as the tool response instead of propagating the error. Returning `undefined` allows the original error to be raised.
    

### onUserMessageCallback[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BasePlugin.html#onusermessagecallback)

    #### Returns Promise<Content | undefined\>
    
    An optional `Content` to be returned to the ADK. Returning a value to replace the user message. Returning `undefined` to proceed normally.

# BaseArtifactService | ADK for TypeScript: API Reference

*   [BaseArtifactService](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/BaseArtifactService.html)
    

Interface BaseArtifactService
=============================

Interface for artifact services.

interface BaseArtifactService {  
    [deleteArtifact](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/BaseArtifactService.html#deleteartifact-1)
(request: [DeleteArtifactRequest](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/DeleteArtifactRequest.html)
): Promise<void\>;  
    [listArtifactKeys](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/BaseArtifactService.html#listartifactkeys-1)
(request: [ListArtifactKeysRequest](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/ListArtifactKeysRequest.html)
): Promise<string\[\]\>;  
    [listVersions](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/BaseArtifactService.html#listversions-1)
(request: [ListVersionsRequest](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/ListVersionsRequest.html)
): Promise<number\[\]\>;  
    [loadArtifact](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/BaseArtifactService.html#loadartifact-1)
(request: [LoadArtifactRequest](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LoadArtifactRequest.html)
): Promise<Part | undefined\>;  
    [saveArtifact](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/BaseArtifactService.html#saveartifact-1)
(request: [SaveArtifactRequest](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/SaveArtifactRequest.html)
): Promise<number\>;  
}

    #### Parameters
    
    *   request: [LoadArtifactRequest](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LoadArtifactRequest.html)

### saveArtifact[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/BaseArtifactService.html#saveartifact)

*   saveArtifact(request: [SaveArtifactRequest](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/SaveArtifactRequest.html)
    ): Promise<number\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/BaseArtifactService.html#saveartifact-1)
    
    Saves an artifact to the artifact service storage.
    
    The artifact is a file identified by the app name, user ID, session ID, and filename. After saving the artifact, a revision ID is returned to identify the artifact version.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   request: [SaveArtifactRequest](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/SaveArtifactRequest.html)
        
        The request to save an artifact.
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<number\>
    
    A promise that resolves to The revision ID. The first version of the artifact has a revision ID of 0. This is incremented by 1 after each successful save.
