# InvocationContext | ADK for TypeScript: API Reference

*   [InvocationContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/InvocationContext.html)
    

Class InvocationContext
=======================

An invocation context represents the data of a single invocation of an agent.

An invocation: 1. Starts with a user message and ends with a final response. 2. Can contain one or multiple agent calls. 3. Is handled by runner.runAsync().

An invocation runs an agent until it does not request to transfer to another agent.

An agent call: 1. Is handled by agent.runAsync(). 2. Ends when agent.runAsync() ends.

An LLM agent call is an agent with a BaseLLMFlow. An LLM agent call can contain one or multiple steps.

An LLM agent runs steps in a loop until:

1.  A final response is generated.
2.  The agent transfers to another agent.
3.  The end\_invocation is set to true by any callbacks or tools.

A step:

1.  Calls the LLM only once and yields its response.
2.  Calls the tools and yields their responses if requested.

The summarization of the function response is considered another step, since it is another llm call. A step ends when it's done calling llm and tools, or if the end\_invocation is set to true at any time.

       ┌─────────────────────── invocation ──────────────────────────┐   ┌──────────── llm_agent_call_1 ────────────┐ ┌─ agent_call_2 ─┐   ┌──── step_1 ────────┐ ┌───── step_2 ──────┐   [call_llm] [call_tool] [call_llm] [transfer]
    

Constructors
------------

### constructor[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/InvocationContext.html#constructor)

*   new InvocationContext(params: InvocationContextParams): [InvocationContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/InvocationContext.html)
    [](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/InvocationContext.html#constructorinvocationcontext)
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   params: InvocationContextParams
        
        The parameters for creating an invocation context.
        
    
    #### Returns [InvocationContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/InvocationContext.html)
    

Properties
----------

### `Optional`activeStreamingTools[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/InvocationContext.html#activestreamingtools)

activeStreamingTools?: Record<string, ActiveStreamingTool\>

The running streaming tools of this invocation.

### agent[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/InvocationContext.html#agent)

agent: [BaseAgent](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseAgent.html)

The current agent of this invocation context.

### `Optional` `Readonly`artifactService[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/InvocationContext.html#artifactservice)

artifactService?: [BaseArtifactService](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/BaseArtifactService.html)

### `Optional`branch[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/InvocationContext.html#branch)

branch?: string

The branch of the invocation context.

The format is like agent\_1.agent\_2.agent\_3, where agent\_1 is the parent of agent\_2, and agent\_2 is the parent of agent\_3.

Branch is used when multiple sub-agents shouldn't see their peer agents' conversation history.

### `Optional` `Readonly`credentialService[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/InvocationContext.html#credentialservice)

credentialService?: [BaseCredentialService](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/BaseCredentialService.html)

### endInvocation[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/InvocationContext.html#endinvocation)

endInvocation: boolean

Whether to end this invocation. Set to True in callbacks or tools to terminate this invocation.

### `Readonly`invocationId[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/InvocationContext.html#invocationid)

invocationId: string

The id of this invocation context.

### `Optional`liveRequestQueue[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/InvocationContext.html#liverequestqueue)

liveRequestQueue?: [LiveRequestQueue](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LiveRequestQueue.html)

### `Optional` `Readonly`memoryService[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/InvocationContext.html#memoryservice)

memoryService?: [BaseMemoryService](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/BaseMemoryService.html)

### pluginManager[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/InvocationContext.html#pluginmanager)

pluginManager: [PluginManager](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/PluginManager.html)

The manager for keeping track of plugins in this invocation.

### `Optional`runConfig[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/InvocationContext.html#runconfig)

runConfig?: [RunConfig](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/RunConfig.html)

### `Readonly`session[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/InvocationContext.html#session)

session: [Session](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Session.html)

### `Optional`transcriptionCache[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/InvocationContext.html#transcriptioncache)

transcriptionCache?: TranscriptionEntry\[\]

Caches necessary, data audio or contents, that are needed by transcription.

### `Optional` `Readonly`userContent[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/InvocationContext.html#usercontent)

# PluginManager | ADK for TypeScript: API Reference

*   [PluginManager](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/PluginManager.html)
    

Class PluginManager
===================

Manages the registration and execution of plugins.

The PluginManager is an internal class that orchestrates the invocation of plugin callbacks at key points in the SDK's execution lifecycle. It maintains a list of registered plugins and ensures they are called in the order they were registered.

The core execution logic implements an "early exit" strategy: if any plugin callback returns a non-`undefined` value, the execution of subsequent plugins for that specific event is halted, and the returned value is propagated up the call stack. This allows plugins to short-circuit operations like agent runs, tool calls, or model requests.

Constructors
------------

### constructor[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/PluginManager.html#constructor)

*   new PluginManager(plugins?: [BasePlugin](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BasePlugin.html)
    \[\]): [PluginManager](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/PluginManager.html)
    [](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/PluginManager.html#constructorpluginmanager)
    
    Initializes the plugin service.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   `Optional`plugins: [BasePlugin](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BasePlugin.html)
        \[\]
        
        An optional list of plugins to register upon initialization.
        
    
    #### Returns [PluginManager](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/PluginManager.html)

# LoggingPlugin | ADK for TypeScript: API Reference

*   [LoggingPlugin](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LoggingPlugin.html)
    

Class LoggingPlugin
===================

A plugin that logs important information at each callback point.

This plugin helps printing all critical events in the console. It is not a replacement of existing logging in ADK. It rather helps terminal based debugging by showing all logs in the console, and serves as a simple demo for everyone to leverage when developing new plugins.

This plugin helps users track the invocation status by logging:

*   User messages and invocation context
*   Agent execution flow
*   LLM requests and responses
*   Tool calls with arguments and results
*   Events and final responses
*   Errors during model and tool execution

Example:

    const loggingPlugin = new LoggingPlugin();const runner = new Runner({  agents: [myAgent],  // ...  plugins: [loggingPlugin],});
    

#### Hierarchy ([View Summary](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/hierarchy.html#LoggingPlugin)
)

*   [BasePlugin](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BasePlugin.html)
    *   LoggingPlugin

Constructors
------------

### constructor[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LoggingPlugin.html#constructor)

*   new LoggingPlugin(name?: string): [LoggingPlugin](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LoggingPlugin.html)
    [](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LoggingPlugin.html#constructorloggingplugin)
    
    Initialize the logging plugin.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   name: string = 'logging\_plugin'
        
        The name of the plugin instance.
        
    
    #### Returns [LoggingPlugin](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LoggingPlugin.html)

----------

### `Readonly`name[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LoggingPlugin.html#name)

name: string

Methods

### afterAgentCallback[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LoggingPlugin.html#afteragentcallback)

*   afterAgentCallback(  
        agent: { agent: [BaseAgent](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseAgent.html)
    ; callbackContext: [CallbackContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/CallbackContext.html)
     },  
    ): Promise<Content | undefined\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LoggingPlugin.html#afteragentcallback-1)
    
    Callback executed after an agent's primary logic has completed.
    
    This callback can be used to inspect, log, or modify the agent's final result before it is returned.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   agent: { agent: [BaseAgent](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseAgent.html)
        ; callbackContext: [CallbackContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/CallbackContext.html)
         }
        
        The agent that has just run.
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<Content | undefined\>
    
    An optional `Content` object. If a value is returned, it will replace the agent's original result. Returning `undefined` uses the original, unmodified result.
    

### afterModelCallback[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LoggingPlugin.html#aftermodelcallback)

*   afterModelCallback(  
        callbackContext: {  
            callbackContext: [CallbackContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/CallbackContext.html)
    ;  
            llmResponse: [LlmResponse](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html)
    ;  
        },  
    ): Promise<[LlmResponse](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html)
     | undefined\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LoggingPlugin.html#aftermodelcallback-1)
    
    Callback executed after a response is received from the model.
    
    This is the ideal place to log model responses, collect metrics on token usage, or perform post-processing on the raw `LlmResponse`.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   callbackContext: { callbackContext: [CallbackContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/CallbackContext.html)
        ; llmResponse: [LlmResponse](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html)
         }
        
        The context for the current agent call.
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<[LlmResponse](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html)
     | undefined\>
    
    An optional value. A non-`undefined` return may be used by the framework to modify or replace the response. Returning `undefined` allows the original response to be used.
    

### afterRunCallback[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LoggingPlugin.html#afterruncallback)

*   afterRunCallback(  
        invocationContext: { invocationContext: [InvocationContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/InvocationContext.html)
     },  
    ): Promise<void\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LoggingPlugin.html#afterruncallback-1)
    
    Callback executed after an ADK runner run has completed.
    
    This is the final callback in the ADK lifecycle, suitable for cleanup, final logging, or reporting tasks.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   invocationContext: { invocationContext: [InvocationContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/InvocationContext.html)
         }
        
        The context for the entire invocation.
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<void\>
    
    undefined
    

### afterToolCallback[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LoggingPlugin.html#aftertoolcallback)

*   afterToolCallback(  
        tool: {  
            result: Record<string, unknown\>;  
            tool: [BaseTool](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseTool.html)
    ;  
            toolArgs: Record<string, unknown\>;  
            toolContext: [ToolContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/ToolContext.html)
    ;  
        },  
    ): Promise<Record<string, unknown\> | undefined\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LoggingPlugin.html#aftertoolcallback-1)
    
    Callback executed after a tool has been called.
    
    This callback allows for inspecting, logging, or modifying the result returned by a tool.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   tool: {  
            result: Record<string, unknown\>;  
            tool: [BaseTool](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseTool.html)
        ;  
            toolArgs: Record<string, unknown\>;  
            toolContext: [ToolContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/ToolContext.html)
        ;  
        }
        
        The tool instance that has just been executed.
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<Record<string, unknown\> | undefined\>
    
    An optional dictionary. If a dictionary is returned, it will **replace** the original result from the tool. This allows for post-processing or altering tool outputs. Returning `undefined` uses the original, unmodified result.
    

### beforeAgentCallback[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LoggingPlugin.html#beforeagentcallback)

*   beforeAgentCallback(  
        agent: { agent: [BaseAgent](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseAgent.html)
    ; callbackContext: [CallbackContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/CallbackContext.html)
     },  
    ): Promise<Content | undefined\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LoggingPlugin.html#beforeagentcallback-1)
    
    Callback executed before an agent's primary logic is invoked.
    
    This callback can be used for logging, setup, or to short-circuit the agent's execution by returning a value.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   agent: { agent: [BaseAgent](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseAgent.html)
        ; callbackContext: [CallbackContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/CallbackContext.html)
         }
        
        The agent that is about to run.
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<Content | undefined\>
    
    An optional `Content` object. If a value is returned, it will bypass the agent's callbacks and its execution, and return this value directly. Returning `undefined` allows the agent to proceed normally.
    

### beforeModelCallback[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LoggingPlugin.html#beforemodelcallback)

*   beforeModelCallback(  
        callbackContext: {  
            callbackContext: [CallbackContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/CallbackContext.html)
    ;  
            llmRequest: [LlmRequest](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmRequest.html)
    ;  
        },  
    ): Promise<[LlmResponse](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html)
     | undefined\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LoggingPlugin.html#beforemodelcallback-1)
    
    Callback executed before a request is sent to the model.
    
    This provides an opportunity to inspect, log, or modify the `LlmRequest` object. It can also be used to implement caching by returning a cached `LlmResponse`, which would skip the actual model call.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   callbackContext: { callbackContext: [CallbackContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/CallbackContext.html)
        ; llmRequest: [LlmRequest](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmRequest.html)
         }
        
        The context for the current agent call.
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<[LlmResponse](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html)
     | undefined\>
    
    An optional value. The interpretation of a non-`undefined` trigger an early exit and returns the response immediately. Returning `undefined` allows the LLM request to proceed normally.
    

### beforeRunCallback[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LoggingPlugin.html#beforeruncallback)

*   beforeRunCallback(  
        invocationContext: { invocationContext: [InvocationContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/InvocationContext.html)
     },  
    ): Promise<Content | undefined\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LoggingPlugin.html#beforeruncallback-1)
    
    Callback executed before the ADK runner runs.
    
    This is the first callback to be called in the lifecycle, ideal for global setup or initialization tasks.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   invocationContext: { invocationContext: [InvocationContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/InvocationContext.html)
         }
        
        The context for the entire invocation, containing session information, the root agent, etc.
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<Content | undefined\>
    
    An optional `Event` to be returned to the ADK. Returning a value to halt execution of the runner and ends the runner with that event. Return `undefined` to proceed normally.
    

### beforeToolCallback[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LoggingPlugin.html#beforetoolcallback)

*   beforeToolCallback(  
        tool: {  
            tool: [BaseTool](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseTool.html)
    ;

    ;  
        },  
    ): Promise<Record<string, unknown\> | undefined\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LoggingPlugin.html#beforetoolcallback-1)
    
    Callback executed before a tool is called.
    
    This callback is useful for logging tool usage, input validation, or modifying the arguments before they are passed to the tool.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   tool: { tool: [BaseTool](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseTool.html)
        ; toolArgs: Record<string, unknown\>; toolContext: [ToolContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/ToolContext.html)
         }
        
        The tool instance that is about to be executed.
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<Record<string, unknown\> | undefined\>
    
    An optional dictionary. If a dictionary is returned, it will stop the tool execution and return this response immediately. Returning `undefined` uses the original, unmodified arguments.
    

### onEventCallback[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LoggingPlugin.html#oneventcallback)

*   onEventCallback(  
        invocationContext: {

    ;

     | undefined\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LoggingPlugin.html#oneventcallback-1)
    
    Callback executed after an event is yielded from runner.
    
    This is the ideal place to make modification to the event before the event is handled by the underlying agent app.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   invocationContext: { event: [Event](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Event.html)
        ; invocationContext: [InvocationContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/InvocationContext.html)
         }
        
        The context for the entire invocation.
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<[Event](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Event.html)
     | undefined\>
    
    An optional value. A non-`undefined` return may be used by the framework to modify or replace the response. Returning `undefined` allows the original response to be used.
    

### onModelErrorCallback[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LoggingPlugin.html#onmodelerrorcallback)

*   onModelErrorCallback(  
        callbackContext: {  
            callbackContext: [CallbackContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/CallbackContext.html)
    ;  
            error: Error;  
            llmRequest: [LlmRequest](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmRequest.html)
    ;  
        },  
    ): Promise<[LlmResponse](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html)
     | undefined\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LoggingPlugin.html#onmodelerrorcallback-1)
    
    Callback executed when a model call encounters an error.
    
    This callback provides an opportunity to handle model errors gracefully, potentially providing alternative responses or recovery mechanisms.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   callbackContext: { callbackContext: [CallbackContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/CallbackContext.html)
        ; error: Error; llmRequest: [LlmRequest](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmRequest.html)
         }
        
        The context for the current agent call.
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<[LlmResponse](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html)
     | undefined\>
    
    An optional LlmResponse. If an LlmResponse is returned, it will be used instead of propagating the error. Returning `undefined` allows the original error to be raised.
    

### onToolErrorCallback[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LoggingPlugin.html#ontoolerrorcallback)

*   onToolErrorCallback(  
        tool: {  
            error: Error;  
            tool: [BaseTool](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseTool.html)
    ;  
            toolArgs: Record<string, unknown\>;  
            toolContext: [ToolContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/ToolContext.html)
    ;  
        },  
    ): Promise<Record<string, unknown\> | undefined\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LoggingPlugin.html#ontoolerrorcallback-1)
    
    Callback executed when a tool call encounters an error.
    
    This callback provides an opportunity to handle tool errors gracefully, potentially providing alternative responses or recovery mechanisms.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   tool: {  
            error: Error;  
            tool: [BaseTool](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseTool.html)
        ;  
            toolArgs: Record<string, unknown\>;  
            toolContext: [ToolContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/ToolContext.html)
        ;  
        }
        
        The tool instance that encountered an error.
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<Record<string, unknown\> | undefined\>
    
    An optional dictionary. If a dictionary is returned, it will be used as the tool response instead of propagating the error. Returning `undefined` allows the original error to be raised.
    

### onUserMessageCallback[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LoggingPlugin.html#onusermessagecallback)

*   onUserMessageCallback(  
        invocationContext: {  
            invocationContext: [InvocationContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/InvocationContext.html)
    ;  
            userMessage: Content;  
        },  
    ): Promise<Content | undefined\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LoggingPlugin.html#onusermessagecallback-1)
    
    Callback executed when a user message is received before an invocation starts.
    
    This callback helps logging and modifying the user message before the runner starts the invocation.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   invocationContext: { invocationContext: [InvocationContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/InvocationContext.html)
        ; userMessage: Content }
        
        The context for the entire invocation.
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<Content | undefined\>
    
    An optional `Content` to be returned to the ADK. Returning a value to replace the user message. Returning `undefined` to proceed normally.

    ): Promise<[LlmResponse](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html)
     | undefined\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/SecurityPlugin.html#aftermodelcallback-1)
    
    Callback executed after a response is received from the model.
    
    This is the ideal place to log model responses, collect metrics on token usage, or perform post-processing on the raw `LlmResponse`.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   callbackContext: { callbackContext: [CallbackContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/CallbackContext.html)
        ; llmResponse: [LlmResponse](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html)
         }
        
        The context for the current agent call.
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<[LlmResponse](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html)
     | undefined\>
    
    An optional value. A non-`undefined` return may be used by the framework to modify or replace the response. Returning `undefined` allows the original response to be used.
    

### afterRunCallback[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/SecurityPlugin.html#afterruncallback)

*   afterRunCallback(  
        invocationContext: { invocationContext: [InvocationContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/InvocationContext.html)
     },  
    ): Promise<void\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/SecurityPlugin.html#afterruncallback-1)
    
    Callback executed after an ADK runner run has completed.
    
    This is the final callback in the ADK lifecycle, suitable for cleanup, final logging, or reporting tasks.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   invocationContext: { invocationContext: [InvocationContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/InvocationContext.html)
         }
        
        The context for the entire invocation.
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<void\>
    
    undefined
    

### afterToolCallback[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/SecurityPlugin.html#aftertoolcallback)

*   afterToolCallback(  
        tool: {  
            result: Record<string, unknown\>;  
            tool: [BaseTool](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseTool.html)
    ;  
            toolArgs: Record<string, unknown\>;  
            toolContext: [ToolContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/ToolContext.html)
    ;  
        },  
    ): Promise<Record<string, unknown\> | undefined\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/SecurityPlugin.html#aftertoolcallback-1)
    
    Callback executed after a tool has been called.
    
    This callback allows for inspecting, logging, or modifying the result returned by a tool.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   tool: {  
            result: Record<string, unknown\>;  
            tool: [BaseTool](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseTool.html)
        ;  
            toolArgs: Record<string, unknown\>;  
            toolContext: [ToolContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/ToolContext.html)
        ;  
        }
        
        The tool instance that has just been executed.
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<Record<string, unknown\> | undefined\>
    
    An optional dictionary. If a dictionary is returned, it will **replace** the original result from the tool. This allows for post-processing or altering tool outputs. Returning `undefined` uses the original, unmodified result.
    

### beforeAgentCallback[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/SecurityPlugin.html#beforeagentcallback)

*   beforeAgentCallback(  
        agent: { agent: [BaseAgent](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseAgent.html)
    ; callbackContext: [CallbackContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/CallbackContext.html)
     },  
    ): Promise<Content | undefined\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/SecurityPlugin.html#beforeagentcallback-1)
    
    Callback executed before an agent's primary logic is invoked.
    
    This callback can be used for logging, setup, or to short-circuit the agent's execution by returning a value.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   agent: { agent: [BaseAgent](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseAgent.html)
        ; callbackContext: [CallbackContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/CallbackContext.html)
         }
        
        The agent that is about to run.
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<Content | undefined\>
    
    An optional `Content` object. If a value is returned, it will bypass the agent's callbacks and its execution, and return this value directly. Returning `undefined` allows the agent to proceed normally.
    

### beforeModelCallback[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/SecurityPlugin.html#beforemodelcallback)

*   beforeModelCallback(  
        callbackContext: {  
            callbackContext: [CallbackContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/CallbackContext.html)
    ;  
            llmRequest: [LlmRequest](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmRequest.html)
    ;  
        },  
    ): Promise<[LlmResponse](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html)
     | undefined\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/SecurityPlugin.html#beforemodelcallback-1)
    
    Callback executed before a request is sent to the model.
    
    This provides an opportunity to inspect, log, or modify the `LlmRequest` object. It can also be used to implement caching by returning a cached `LlmResponse`, which would skip the actual model call.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   callbackContext: { callbackContext: [CallbackContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/CallbackContext.html)
        ; llmRequest: [LlmRequest](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmRequest.html)
         }
        
        The context for the current agent call.
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<[LlmResponse](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html)
     | undefined\>
    
    An optional value. The interpretation of a non-`undefined` trigger an early exit and returns the response immediately. Returning `undefined` allows the LLM request to proceed normally.
    

### beforeRunCallback[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/SecurityPlugin.html#beforeruncallback)

*   beforeRunCallback(  
        invocationContext: { invocationContext: [InvocationContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/InvocationContext.html)
     },  
    ): Promise<Content | undefined\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/SecurityPlugin.html#beforeruncallback-1)
    
    Callback executed before the ADK runner runs.
    
    This is the first callback to be called in the lifecycle, ideal for global setup or initialization tasks.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   invocationContext: { invocationContext: [InvocationContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/InvocationContext.html)
         }
        
        The context for the entire invocation, containing session information, the root agent, etc.
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<Content | undefined\>
    
    An optional `Event` to be returned to the ADK. Returning a value to halt execution of the runner and ends the runner with that event. Return `undefined` to proceed normally.
    

### beforeToolCallback[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/SecurityPlugin.html#beforetoolcallback)

*   beforeToolCallback(  
        tool: {  
            tool: [BaseTool](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseTool.html)
    ;  
            toolArgs: { \[key: string\]: unknown };  
            toolContext: [ToolContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/ToolContext.html)
    ;  
        },  
    ): Promise<{ \[key: string\]: unknown } | undefined\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/SecurityPlugin.html#beforetoolcallback-1)
    
    Callback executed before a tool is called.
    
    This callback is useful for logging tool usage, input validation, or modifying the arguments before they are passed to the tool.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   tool: {  
            tool: [BaseTool](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseTool.html)
        ;  
            toolArgs: { \[key: string\]: unknown };  
            toolContext: [ToolContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/ToolContext.html)
        ;  
        }
        
        The tool instance that is about to be executed.
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<{ \[key: string\]: unknown } | undefined\>
    
    An optional dictionary. If a dictionary is returned, it will stop the tool execution and return this response immediately. Returning `undefined` uses the original, unmodified arguments.
    

### onEventCallback[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/SecurityPlugin.html#oneventcallback)

*   onEventCallback(  
        invocationContext: {  
            event: [Event](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Event.html)
    ;  
            invocationContext: [InvocationContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/InvocationContext.html)
    ;  
        },  
    ): Promise<[Event](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Event.html)
     | undefined\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/SecurityPlugin.html#oneventcallback-1)
    
    Callback executed after an event is yielded from runner.
    
    This is the ideal place to make modification to the event before the event is handled by the underlying agent app.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   invocationContext: { event: [Event](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Event.html)
        ; invocationContext: [InvocationContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/InvocationContext.html)
         }
        
        The context for the entire invocation.
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<[Event](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/Event.html)
     | undefined\>
    
    An optional value. A non-`undefined` return may be used by the framework to modify or replace the response. Returning `undefined` allows the original response to be used.
    

### onModelErrorCallback[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/SecurityPlugin.html#onmodelerrorcallback)

*   onModelErrorCallback(  
        callbackContext: {  
            callbackContext: [CallbackContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/CallbackContext.html)
    ;  
            error: Error;  
            llmRequest: [LlmRequest](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmRequest.html)
    ;  
        },  
    ): Promise<[LlmResponse](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html)
     | undefined\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/SecurityPlugin.html#onmodelerrorcallback-1)
    
    Callback executed when a model call encounters an error.
    
    This callback provides an opportunity to handle model errors gracefully, potentially providing alternative responses or recovery mechanisms.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   callbackContext: { callbackContext: [CallbackContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/CallbackContext.html)
        ; error: Error; llmRequest: [LlmRequest](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmRequest.html)
         }
        
        The context for the current agent call.
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<[LlmResponse](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/interfaces/LlmResponse.html)
     | undefined\>
    
    An optional LlmResponse. If an LlmResponse is returned, it will be used instead of propagating the error. Returning `undefined` allows the original error to be raised.
    

### onToolErrorCallback[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/SecurityPlugin.html#ontoolerrorcallback)

*   onToolErrorCallback(  
        tool: {  
            error: Error;  
            tool: [BaseTool](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseTool.html)
    ;  
            toolArgs: Record<string, unknown\>;  
            toolContext: [ToolContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/ToolContext.html)
    ;  
        },  
    ): Promise<Record<string, unknown\> | undefined\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/SecurityPlugin.html#ontoolerrorcallback-1)
    
    Callback executed when a tool call encounters an error.
    
    This callback provides an opportunity to handle tool errors gracefully, potentially providing alternative responses or recovery mechanisms.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   tool: {  
            error: Error;  
            tool: [BaseTool](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseTool.html)
        ;  
            toolArgs: Record<string, unknown\>;  
            toolContext: [ToolContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/ToolContext.html)
        ;  
        }
        
        The tool instance that encountered an error.
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<Record<string, unknown\> | undefined\>
    
    An optional dictionary. If a dictionary is returned, it will be used as the tool response instead of propagating the error. Returning `undefined` allows the original error to be raised.
    

### onUserMessageCallback[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/SecurityPlugin.html#onusermessagecallback)

*   onUserMessageCallback(  
        invocationContext: {  
            invocationContext: [InvocationContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/InvocationContext.html)
    ;  
            userMessage: Content;  
        },  
    ): Promise<Content | undefined\>[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/SecurityPlugin.html#onusermessagecallback-1)
    
    Callback executed when a user message is received before an invocation starts.
    
    This callback helps logging and modifying the user message before the runner starts the invocation.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   invocationContext: { invocationContext: [InvocationContext](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/InvocationContext.html)
        ; userMessage: Content }
        
        The context for the entire invocation.
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<Content | undefined\>
    
    An optional `Content` to be returned to the ADK. Returning a value to replace the user message. Returning `undefined` to proceed normally.

# LlmAgent | ADK for TypeScript: API Reference

*   [LlmAgent](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LlmAgent.html)
    

Class LlmAgent
==============

An agent that uses a large language model to generate responses.

#### Hierarchy ([View Summary](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/hierarchy.html#LlmAgent)
)

*   [BaseAgent](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseAgent.html)
    *   LlmAgent

Constructors
------------

### constructor[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LlmAgent.html#constructor)

*   new LlmAgent(config: LlmAgentConfig): [LlmAgent](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LlmAgent.html)
    [](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LlmAgent.html#constructorllmagent)
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   config: LlmAgentConfig
    
    #### Returns [LlmAgent](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LlmAgent.html)
    

Properties
----------

### `Readonly`afterAgentCallback[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LlmAgent.html#afteragentcallback)

afterAgentCallback: SingleAgentCallback\[\]

Callback or list of callbacks to be invoked after the agent run.

When a list of callbacks is provided, the callbacks will be called in the order they are listed until a callback does not return undefined.

#### Param: callbackContext:[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LlmAgent.html#param-callbackcontext)

MUST be named 'callbackContext' (enforced).

#### Returns[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LlmAgent.html#returns)

Content: The content to return to the user. When the content is present, the provided content will be used as agent response and appended to event history as agent response.

### `Optional`afterModelCallback[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LlmAgent.html#aftermodelcallback)

afterModelCallback?: [AfterModelCallback](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/types/AfterModelCallback.html)

### `Optional`afterToolCallback[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LlmAgent.html#aftertoolcallback)

afterToolCallback?: [AfterToolCallback](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/types/AfterToolCallback.html)

### `Readonly`beforeAgentCallback[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LlmAgent.html#beforeagentcallback)

beforeAgentCallback: SingleAgentCallback\[\]

Callback or list of callbacks to be invoked before the agent run.

When a list of callbacks is provided, the callbacks will be called in the order they are listed until a callback does not return undefined.

#### Param: callbackContext:[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LlmAgent.html#param-callbackcontext-1)

MUST be named 'callbackContext' (enforced).

#### Returns[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LlmAgent.html#returns-1)

Content: The content to return to the user. When the content is present, the agent run will be skipped and the provided content will be returned to user.

### `Optional`beforeModelCallback[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LlmAgent.html#beforemodelcallback)

beforeModelCallback?: [BeforeModelCallback](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/types/BeforeModelCallback.html)

### `Optional`beforeToolCallback[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LlmAgent.html#beforetoolcallback)

beforeToolCallback?: [BeforeToolCallback](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/types/BeforeToolCallback.html)

### `Optional` `Readonly`description[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LlmAgent.html#description)

description?: string

Description about the agent's capability.

The model uses this to determine whether to delegate control to the agent. One-line description is enough and preferred.

### disallowTransferToParent[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LlmAgent.html#disallowtransfertoparent)

disallowTransferToParent: boolean

### disallowTransferToPeers[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LlmAgent.html#disallowtransfertopeers)

disallowTransferToPeers: boolean

### `Optional`generateContentConfig[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LlmAgent.html#generatecontentconfig)

generateContentConfig?: GenerateContentConfig

### globalInstruction[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LlmAgent.html#globalinstruction)

globalInstruction: string | InstructionProvider

### includeContents[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LlmAgent.html#includecontents)

includeContents: "none" | "default"

### `Optional`inputSchema[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LlmAgent.html#inputschema)

inputSchema?: Schema

### instruction[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LlmAgent.html#instruction)

instruction: string | InstructionProvider

### `Optional`model[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LlmAgent.html#model)

model?: string | [BaseLlm](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseLlm.html)

### `Readonly`name[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LlmAgent.html#name)

name: string

The agent's name. Agent name must be a JS identifier and unique within the agent tree. Agent name cannot be "user", since it's reserved for end-user's input.

### `Optional`outputKey[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LlmAgent.html#outputkey)

outputKey?: string

### `Optional`outputSchema[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LlmAgent.html#outputschema)

outputSchema?: Schema

### `Optional`parentAgent[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LlmAgent.html#parentagent)

parentAgent?: [BaseAgent](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseAgent.html)

The parent agent of this agent.

Note that an agent can ONLY be added as sub-agent once.

If you want to add one agent twice as sub-agent, consider to create two agent instances with identical config, but with different name and add them to the agent tree.

The parent agent is the agent that created this agent.

### requestProcessors[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LlmAgent.html#requestprocessors)

requestProcessors: BaseLlmRequestProcessor\[\]

### responseProcessors[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LlmAgent.html#responseprocessors)

responseProcessors: BaseLlmResponseProcessor\[\]

rootAgent: [BaseAgent](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseAgent.html)

Root agent of this agent.

subAgents: [BaseAgent](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseAgent.html)
\[\]

The sub-agents of this agent.

    \[\]
    
    The resolved self.before\_tool\_callback field as a list of BeforeToolCallback.
    
    This method is only for use by Agent Development Kit.
    
    #### Returns [SingleBeforeToolCallback](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/types/SingleBeforeToolCallback.html)
    \[\]
    

### canonicalModel[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/LlmAgent.html#canonicalmodel)

*   get canonicalModel(): [BaseLlm](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/api-reference/typescript/classes/BaseLlm.html)

    When not set, the agent will inherit the model from its ancestor.
